speaker: Unknown
en: It is really important to me that we feel that we've deliberately understaffed every project at the company.
zh: 对我来说，我们要感觉公司里的每一个项目都是故意人手不足的，这一点真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you overstaff, you get politics, you get people working on things that are further down the priority list than necessary.
zh: 如果你人手过剩，就会产生政治斗争，人们就会去处理那些优先级本不必要那么低的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is poison.
zh: 那是有毒的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's wasteful.
zh: 那是浪费。

speaker: Unknown
en: It slows you down.
zh: 那会拖慢你的速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: It creates cruft.
zh: 那会产生垃圾代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've been a long time COO at Rippling.
zh: 你在 Rippling 担任了很长时间的 COO。

speaker: Unknown
en: Recently, you moved into CPO, Chief Product Officer at Rippling.
zh: 最近，你转岗担任了 Rippling 的 CPO，即首席产品官。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something you talk a lot about is that extraordinary results require extraordinary efforts.
zh: 你经常谈到的一件事是，非凡的成果需要非凡的努力。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to be in the 99th percentile in terms of outcomes, it's going to be really difficult.
zh: 如果你想要在成果上处于前 1% 的百分位，那将会非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to sort of remind people that if they ever find themselves in the comfort zone at work, they are definitely making a mistake.
zh: 你必须得提醒人们，如果他们在工作中发现自己处于舒适区，那他们肯定是在犯错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's supposed to be really fricking exhausting.
zh: 这本应该是极其令人精疲力竭的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a big fan of escalating issues.
zh: 你非常推崇将问题升级。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fundamentally, the most selfish thing you can do is withhold feedback from someone.
zh: 从根本上说，你能做的最自私的事情就是扣留对别人的反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you think a thought that would help someone improve and you avoid giving it to them because it would make you uncomfortable.
zh: 当你有一个想法可以帮助某人改进，但你却因为让你感到不舒服而不把它给他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, you're optimizing for your own comfort, and it's fundamentally selfish.
zh: 好吧，你是在为你自己的舒适而优化，这从根本上说是自私的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So many people have teams that are not functioning incredibly well.
zh: 很多人拥有的团队运作得并不是特别好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teams will always optimize for local comfort over company outcomes.
zh: 团队总是会为了局部的舒适度而优化，而不是为了公司的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: The purest form of ambition and most intense source of energy in the business is the founder CEO.
zh: 商业中最纯粹的雄心壮志和最强烈的能量来源是创始人 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every next concentric circle of management beyond the founder CEO has the potential to be an order of magnitude drop off in intensity.
zh: 创始人 CEO 之外的每一圈管理层，其强度都有可能出现一个数量级的下降。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is fucking dangerous.
zh: 那是非常危险的。

speaker: Unknown
en: As an executive, as a leader, your job is to preserve that intensity at its highest possible level.
zh: 作为一名高管，作为一名领导者，你的工作就是尽可能以最高水平保持这种强度。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've had a couple really interesting experiences with your own startup.
zh: 你自己的初创公司有过一些非常有趣的经历。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talk in Silicon Valley about never quit, but that is complete absolute venture capital.
zh: 我们在硅谷谈论永不放弃，但这完全是绝对的风险投资。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Matt MacInnis, Chief Product Officer and formerly longtime Chief Operating Officer at Rippling.
zh: 今天我的嘉宾是 Matt MacInnis，他是 Rippling 的首席产品官，也是前任长期的首席运营官。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you don't know much about Rippling, it's a massively successful business last valued at over $16 billion.
zh: 如果你不怎么了解 Rippling，这是一家规模巨大的成功企业，最后的估值超过 160 亿美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have over 5,000 employees, and Matt has been instrumental to that success.
zh: 他们拥有超过 5,000 名员工，Matt 对这一成功起到了关键作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's also got a really rare combination of brutal honesty, a ton of experience building a very complex and very successful business, and being able to clearly articulate what he has learned really well.
zh: 他还拥有一种非常罕见的组合，即残酷的诚实、在构建非常复杂和非常成功业务方面的丰富经验，以及能够非常清楚地表达他所学到的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Matt shared a lot of insights and advice that I've not heard anyone else on this podcast share, and I left this conversation feeling that every leader needs to hear his advice.
zh: Matt 分享了很多见解和建议，我在这个播客的其他嘉宾那里从未听到过，我在结束这次谈话时觉得，每一位领导者都需要听听他的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: A huge thank you to Albert Scrashim and Sunil Raman for suggesting topics and questions for this conversation.
zh: 非常感谢 Albert Scrashim 和 Sunil Raman 为这次对话建议的主题和问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，别忘了在你最喜欢的播客应用程序或 YouTube 上订阅和关注它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps tremendously.
zh: 这帮助极大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you become an annual subscriber of my newsletter, you get a year free of 19 incredible products, an entire year of Lovable, Replit, Bolt, Gamma, Inata, Linear, Devin, PostHog, Superhuman, descript, Whisperful, Perplexity, warp, granola, Magic Patterns, Raycast, ChatPARD, Mobbin, and Stripe Atlas.
zh: 如果你成为我年度简报的年度订阅者，你将免费获得一年 19 种令人难以置信的产品，整整一年的 Lovable、Replit、Bolt、Gamma、Inata、Linear、Devin、PostHog、Superhuman、descript、Whisperful、Perplexity、warp、granola、Magic Patterns、Raycast、ChatPARD、Mobbin 和 Stripe Atlas。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head on over to Lenny'snewsletter.com and click product pass.
zh: 请前往 Lenny'snewsletter.com 并点击 product pass。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Matt MacInnis after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 话说到此，在插播赞助商的简短广告后，我为您带来 Matt MacInnis。

speaker: Unknown
en: This podcast is sponsored by Google.
zh: 本播客由 Google 赞助。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hey folks, I'm Amar, product and design lead at Google DeepMind.
zh: 嘿大家，我是 Amar，Google DeepMind 的产品和设计负责人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Have you ever wanted to build an app for yourself, your friends, or finally launch that side project you've been dreaming about?
zh: 你有没有想过为你自己、你的朋友们构建一个应用程序，或者是最终启动你梦想已久的副业项目？

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you can bring any idea to life, no coding background required with Gemini three in Google AI Studio.
zh: 现在你可以把任何想法变为现实，无需具备编码背景，这得益于 Google AI Studio 中的 Gemini three。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's called vibe coding and we're making it dead simple.
zh: 这被称为 vibe coding，我们正在让它变得极其简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just describe your app and Gemini will wire up the right models for you so you can focus on your creative vision.
zh: 只需描述你的应用程序，Gemini 就会为你连接合适的模型，这样你就可以专注于你的创意愿景。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head to ai.studio/build to create your first app.
zh: 请前往 ai.studio/build 创建你的第一个应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Datadog, now home to Eppo, the leading experimentation and feature flagging platform.
zh: 本集由 Datadog 为您带来，它现在是 Eppo 的所在地，Eppo 是领先的实验和功能标记平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Product managers at the world's best companies use Datadog, the same platform their engineers rely on every day to connect product insights to product issues like bugs, UX friction, and business impact.
zh: 全球最优秀公司的产品经理使用 Datadog，这是他们的工程师每天依赖的同一平台，用于将产品洞察与产品问题（如错误、UX 摩擦和商业影响）联系起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It starts with product analytics where PMs can watch replays, review funnels, dive into retention and explore their growth metrics.
zh: 它始于产品分析，产品经理可以在其中观看回放、审查漏斗、深入研究留存率并探索他们的增长指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where other tools stop, Datadog goes even further.
zh: 在其他工具止步的地方，Datadog 走得更远。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps you actually diagnose the impact of funnel drop-offs and bugs and UX friction.
zh: 它实际上帮助你诊断漏斗流失、错误和 UX 摩擦的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once you know where to focus, experiments prove what works.
zh: 一旦你知道了重点在哪里，实验就会证明什么有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I saw this firsthand when I was at Airbnb where our experimentation platform was critical for analyzing what worked and where things went wrong.
zh: 我在 Airbnb 时亲眼目睹了这一点，当时我们的实验平台对于分析什么有效以及哪里出了问题是至关重要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the same team that built experimentation at Airbnb built Eppo.
zh: 而在 Airbnb 构建实验平台的同一个团队构建了 Eppo。

speaker: Unknown
en: Datadog then lets you go beyond the numbers with session replay.
zh: Datadog 让你可以超越数字，利用会话回放进行深入分析。

speaker: Unknown
en: Watch exactly how users interact with heat maps and scroll maps to truly understand their behavior.
zh: 通过热力图和滚动图精准观察用户交互方式，真正理解他们的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of this is powered by feature flags that are tied to real-time data so that you can roll out safely, target precisely and learn continuously.
zh: 所有这些功能都由特性标志驱动，并关联实时数据，让你安全发布、精准定位并持续学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: Datadog is more than engineering metrics.
zh: Datadog 不仅仅是工程指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's where great product teams learn faster, fix smarter, and ship with confidence.
zh: 它是优秀产品团队更快学习、更智能修复并充满信心交付产品的平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Request a demo at datadoghq.com/lenny.
zh: 请访问 datadoghq.com/lenny 申请演示。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's datadoghq.com/lenny.
zh: 网址是 datadoghq.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Matt, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Matt，非常感谢你来到这里，欢迎参加播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you for having me.
zh: 谢谢你的邀请。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to start with something that I know is really important to you, something you talk a lot about that I don't think people hear enough on podcasts like this, which is that extraordinary results require extraordinary efforts.
zh: 我想从一件对你来说非常重要的事情开始，这也是你经常谈论的，我认为在这类播客中人们听得不够多，那就是非凡的成果需要非凡的努力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about why this is so important, what you think people need to hear.
zh: 谈谈为什么这如此重要，你认为人们需要听到什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a term, that phrasing I actually attribute to a friend of mine, Dan Gill, who's the chief product officer at Carvana, which as a company also doesn't get enough credit for how much of a tech company it actually is.
zh: 这个术语，这个表述我实际上归功于我的朋友 Dan Gill，他是 Carvana 的首席产品官，作为一家公司，它实际上作为一家科技公司也没有得到足够的认可。

speaker: Unknown
en: Super interesting.
zh: 非常有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think as a general framework for me, and a lot of what I say with you today is not really specific to product in any way.
zh: 我认为作为我的一个通用框架，我今天跟你说的很多内容其实并不是专门针对产品的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We should actually talk about that.
zh: 我们实际上应该谈谈这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like the product function is an instantiation of the general concept of management.
zh: 就像产品职能是管理这个通用概念的一个实例一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Being a chief product officer is not that different from being a chief whatever officer.
zh: 作为首席产品官和担任其他任何首席官并没有太大不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to apply the same frameworks and concepts to get people to achieve goals together.
zh: 你必须应用相同的框架和概念来让人们一起实现目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: But one thing that is absolutely universal that I think we, honestly, I think we forget it in Silicon Valley or a lot of people don't sort of internalize it, is that if you want to accomplish something truly extraordinary, if you want to be in the 99th percentile in terms of outcomes, it's going to be really difficult.
zh: 但有一件事是绝对普遍适用的，老实说，我认为我们在硅谷忘记了这一点，或者很多人没有真正内化它，那就是如果你想成就真正非凡的事情，如果你想获得前 1% 的结果，那将会非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be really uncomfortable.
zh: 那会让人非常不舒服。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you got to sort of remind people of that, that if they ever find themselves in the comfort zone at work, they are definitely making a mistake.
zh: 你必须不断提醒人们这一点，如果他们在工作中发现自己处于舒适区，那他们肯定犯了错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have definitely screwed up somehow.
zh: 他们肯定在某个地方搞砸了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that an extraordinary effort is sufficient to an extraordinary outcome, but it is 100% true that it is necessary.
zh: 这并不是说非凡的努力就足以带来非凡的结果，但 100% 的事实是，它是必要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I do use that framework as a sort of guiding principle in my own leadership.
zh: 所以我确实把这个框架作为我自己领导作风中的一个指导原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: To make this even more real for people, what are examples of moments that were extraordinarily hard?
zh: 为了让人们对此有更切身的体会，有哪些极其艰难时刻的例子？

speaker: Unknown
en: It is not about any sort of grand single story.
zh: 这不是关于任何宏大的单一故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the story is actually told through a thousand little things.
zh: 我认为故事实际上是通过一千件小事讲述的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so for me, the story is told through a thousand Jira tickets, not through a thousand grand events.
zh: 所以对我来说，故事是通过一千个 Jira 工单讲述的，而不是通过一千个宏大事件。

speaker: Unknown
en: The extraordinary effort thing is a reminder that it's supposed to be really fricking exhausting.
zh: 非凡努力这件事是在提醒我们，这过程本应该是极其令人筋疲力尽的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's supposed to be.
zh: 它本就是这样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So on Friday night, when you get hit with an escalation on Friday night, when you get sort of hit with a bunch of new bugs from someone in the engineering team that you've got a triage, those are the moments where great players and great teams are separated from good players and good teams.
zh: 所以在周五晚上，当你接到升级处理时，当你收到来自工程团队的一堆新 Bug 需要你去分诊时，这些时刻就是优秀球员和优秀球队与良好球员和良好球队被区分开来的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's so easy to say this at a company like Rippling because we're winning.
zh: 在像 Rippling 这样的公司说这些很容易，因为我们正在赢。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a company, for all of our foibles, and we should spend time today talking about where things are not perfect and not great, but the growth rate of the company on the revenue foundation that we have is extraordinary, really, really compelling.
zh: 作为一家公司，尽管我们有各种缺点，我们今天应该花时间谈谈哪些地方不完美、不好，但公司在我们现有的营收基础上的增长率是非凡的，真的、非常引人注目。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it gives you, as a leader, the air cover to get up in front of your team and say, "Hey guys, I need the last ounce of oil that you've got left."
zh: 它作为领导者给了你空中掩护，让你能站在团队面前说：“嘿伙计们，我需要你们拿出最后的一丝精力。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And if your company's not growing very quickly, if things aren't that great, if your growth rate is 30% or 40%, it doesn't feel as good as a contributor in that business to lean in and give everything you've got on Friday or Saturday or Sunday because you don't know that it's going to yield much.
zh: 如果你的公司增长不是很快，如果情况不是那么好，如果你的增长率是 30% 或 40%，作为该业务的贡献者在周五、周六或周日全身心投入并付出一切的感觉就不会那么好，因为你不知道这会带来多少回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so extraordinary results, outcomes demand extraordinary efforts, but if there's no chance at an extraordinary outcome, it's very hard to get the extraordinary effort.
zh: 所以非凡的结果、结果需要非凡的努力，但如果没有非凡结果的机会，就很难获得非凡的努力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I like to remind people at Rippling at least that it's so rare to have the opportunity to be able to be a part of a team where the extraordinary effort that you do put in on Friday or whatever, whenever it is actually contributing to an extraordinary result.
zh: 所以我喜欢至少提醒 Rippling 的人，能够有机会成为这样一个团队的一员是非常罕见的，在这个团队里你在周五或其他任何时间投入的非凡努力实际上正在为一个非凡的结果做出贡献。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a very special and rare thing, and it gives me a superpower as a leader because I can lean on that when I'm ringing the oil out of somebody who's in the bored and tired zone.
zh: 这是一件非常特别和罕见的事情，它给了我一种超能力作为领导者，因为当我从某个处于厌倦和疲劳状态的人身上榨取精力时，我可以依靠这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I saw the same thing actually at Airbnb with Brian Chesky.
zh: 我实际上在 Airbnb 和 Brian Chesky 身上也看到了同样的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: It always felt like things were going great and maybe we could take a break after something we shipped was killing it.
zh: 感觉事情总是进展顺利，也许在我们发布的产品大获成功后我们可以休息一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it always felt like the opposite.
zh: 但感觉总是相反的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It always felt like, how do we press the gas pedal further?
zh: 感觉总是，我们要如何进一步踩下油门？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we go faster?
zh: 我们如何走得更快？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we go bigger?
zh: 我们如何做得更大？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's never a moment to take a break.
zh: 从来没有休息的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: I spent seven years at Apple and learned under Steve Jobs when he was the CEO, learned what we called the death march, which is what we did to the engineers.
zh: 我在 Apple 待了七年，在 Steve Jobs 担任 CEO 时在他手下学习，学到了我们所说的“死亡行军”，这就是我们对工程师所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like as soon as you shipped one version of the iPhone, you were just immediately thrown into the pit of building the next one and there was no break.
zh: 就像一旦你发布了 iPhone 的一个版本，你就立即被扔进构建下一个版本的坑里，没有任何休息。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was just relentless and talk about an extraordinary outcome at the end of the day.
zh: 这就是无情的，并且谈论一天结束时非凡的成果。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is no relief.
zh: 没有缓解。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a competitive market, and if the market is valuable, it's competitive, no question.
zh: 在竞争激烈的市场中，如果市场是有价值的，它就是竞争激烈的，毫无疑问。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you leave anything on the field, if you sort of leave a crack for your competitor, 100% chance they're going to go fill that crack.
zh: 如果你在场上留下了任何东西，如果你某种程度上为你的竞争对手留了一条裂缝，他们有 100% 的几率会去填满那条裂缝。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you have to be relentless.
zh: 所以你必须毫不留情。

speaker: Unknown
en: There can be no relaxation of the organization.
zh: 组织不能有任何松懈。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't mean people can't come and go or people can't take vacations or live their lives, of course.
zh: 这当然并不意味着人们不能来来去去，或者人们不能去度假或过他们的生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not like people are human beings.
zh: 并不是说人不是人类。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't grind the individuals down, but the team as a collective group of people has to be sort of on the ball all the time.
zh: 你不能压垮个人，但作为一个集体群体的团队必须时刻保持警惕。

speaker: Unknown
en: There can't be a break.
zh: 不能有间断。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you leave one, you're just begging for the slightly more hungry competitor to come in and eat your lunch.
zh: 如果你留下一个，你简直就是在乞求那个稍微更饥渴的竞争对手进来吃掉你的午餐。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's the beauty of capitalism.
zh: 这就是资本主义的美好之处。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, very counterintuitively, and maybe the more optimistic perspective here is when you do give your team space to just twiddle their thumbs, bad things start to happen.
zh: 而且，非常反直觉的是，也许这里更乐观的观点是，当你确实给你的团队空间去无所事事时，坏事就开始发生了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Morale actually dips in my experience.
zh: 根据我的经验，士气实际上会低落。

speaker: Unknown
en: People get distracted.
zh: 人们会分心。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Oh, what are we even doing?
zh: 他们会想，“噢，我们到底在做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not interesting."
zh: 这没意思。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I find that keeping people busy and motivated and fired up, even though you may think they'll be happier taking a many week break and slowing things down, I find they get more, the more I actually goes down in those moments.
zh: 我发现让人们保持忙碌、充满动力和热情，即使你可能认为他们会在休一个长假、慢下来之后更快乐，但我发现他们获得的更多，实际上我在这些时刻得到的更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So here's a management framework that I use fairly often.
zh: 所以这里有一个我经常使用的管理框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: As an executive, you don't know how to get any decision exactly right.
zh: 作为一个高管，你不知道如何做出任何完全正确的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not knowable.
zh: 这是不可知的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't know how much budget to allocate.
zh: 你不知道要分配多少预算。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't know how many people to put on a project.
zh: 你不知道要在一个项目上安排多少人。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't know how to set a deadline for when you're going to ship something.
zh: 你不知道如何为发布某样东西设定最后期限。

speaker: Unknown
en: But of course, you have to set some default so you make your best guess and then you manage to that best guess and you learn as you go because in software development and in business in general, everything's emergent.
zh: 但当然，你必须设定一些默认值，所以你做出最好的猜测，然后你根据那个最好的猜测进行管理，并且你在过程中学习，因为在软件开发和一般的商业中，一切都是涌现的。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are not things that are knowable top down or a priority.
zh: 这些不是自上而下可知的事情或优先事项。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you take a best guess and knowing that you're not going to get the right answer, you need to decide whether over-steering or under-steering relative to your perceived midpoint is better.
zh: 所以你采取最好的猜测，并且知道你不会得到正确的答案，你需要决定相对于你感知的中点，过度转向还是转向不足更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so let's talk about staffing.
zh: 让我们来谈谈人员配置。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you staff a project, is it better to overstaff or is it better to under-staff knowing that you can't get it right?
zh: 当你为一个项目配置人员时，考虑到你无法做到恰到好处，是过度配置更好，还是配置不足更好？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, it's better to under-staff.
zh: 嗯，配置不足更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you overstaff, you get everything that you just said.
zh: 如果你过度配置，你会得到你刚才说的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: You get politics, you get people working, I think most importantly on things that are further down the priority list than necessary.
zh: 你会得到政治，你会得到人们在，我认为最重要的是在比必要更低的优先级的事情上工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have like 20 things on a stack rank list and you know that you got to do the top five, but the next 15 data's kind of ambiguous, but you've overstaffed the project.
zh: 你有一个堆栈排名列表上有 20 件事，你知道你必须做前五件，但接下来的 15 件事的数据有点模棱两可，但是你过度配置了这个项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the next 10 things down are getting worked on.
zh: 所以下面的 10 件事正在被处理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before you even know if they're necessary, that is poison.
zh: 在你甚至知道它们是否有必要之前，这就是毒药。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's wasteful, it slows you down, it creates crust.
zh: 这是浪费的，它让你慢下来，它产生了结壳。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's very clear that under-staffing is less evil than over-staffing.
zh: 所以很清楚，人员不足比人员过剩要小恶得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: In this particular framework, the advice is under-staff deliberately, always.
zh: 在这个特定的框架中，建议是故意人员不足，总是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the wisdom, the wisdom element is to know not to under-under-staff and sort of knowing the difference between those two things.
zh: 然后智慧，智慧要素在于知道不要过少配置人员，并且知道这两者之间的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's the way we work at Rippling.
zh: 这就是我们在 Rippling 工作的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone is constantly asking for more resources and of course where we can afford to and where it's appropriate new resources arrive, but it is really important to me that we feel that we've deliberately understaffed every project at the company.
zh: 每个人都在不断地要求更多的资源，当然在我们负担得起且合适的地方，新资源会出现，但对我来说非常重要的是，我们要觉得我们故意让公司的每个项目都人手不足。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a previous guest, I forget who this was.
zh: 有一位之前的嘉宾，我忘了这是谁。

speaker: Unknown
en: They used this metaphor if they want their team to be dehydrated to always be wanting more water.
zh: 他们用过这个比喻，如果他们希望他们的团队脱水，总是想要更多的水。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then eventually they're too dehydrated and okay, we needed someone to help.
zh: 然后最终他们太脱水了，好吧，我们需要有人来帮忙。

speaker: Unknown
en: Interesting.
zh: 有意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a line along the lines of extraordinary efforts I want to make sure I read because I think this is really good.
zh: 有这样一句话，关于非凡的努力，我想确保我读一下，因为我认为这真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: This may be a way to summarize what you're saying, that good teams get tired and that's when great teams kick the good team's asses.
zh: 这可能是总结你所说的一种方式，好的团队会累，而这就是伟大的团队痛扁好团队的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was a quote actually from Sunil, and he found it from a women's basketball team coach.
zh: 这实际上是来自 Sunil 的一句引用，他是从一位女子篮球队教练那里找到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it is, to my point earlier about you got to run the engine at the red line at all times because the minute you let your guard down, the minute you slow down, the minute you relax, the minute you leave a crack for your competition, the great teams are going to come in and kick the good team's.
zh: 它就是，关于我之前的一点，你必须时刻让发动机在红线上运转，因为当你放下戒备的那一刻，你慢下来的那一刻，你放松的那一刻，你为你的竞争留下裂缝的那一刻，伟大的团队就会进来痛扁好团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like sports, I'm not a very sporty guy, but sports analogies are sort of irresistible because at the end of the day, business is a game and none of this matters.
zh: 就像体育一样，我不是一个很爱运动的人，但体育的比喻是不可抗拒的，因为在归根结底，商业是一场游戏，这些都不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not going to carry it to the grave.
zh: 我们不会把它带进坟墓。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like you're here to do this stuff because it somehow fulfills you while you're on the planet.
zh: 就像你在这里做这些事是因为它在某种程度上在你身处这个星球时充实了你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love the sport of business and I find that sports, notwithstanding the fact that I watched very little of it, that military, those are very ripe sources of parallel concepts to apply in leadership.
zh: 我热爱商业这项运动，我发现虽然我观看得很少，但体育运动，军事，是将平行概念应用于领导的非常丰富的来源。

speaker: Unknown
en: I find also those most intense, stressful, long nights are the moments you remember most and remember most fondly back to when you're building something.
zh: 我也发现那些最紧张、压力最大、漫长的夜晚是你最难忘且回忆起来最美好的时刻，当你回首往事时。

speaker: Unknown
en: The key though is that it has to go well.
zh: 但关键是事情必须进展顺利。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, if you are succeeding and winning, all of this is romantic in the end and nostalgic.
zh: 正如你所说，如果你正在成功和获胜，这一切最终都是浪漫的和怀旧的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Remember that time we built this thing and worked late nights and shipped this thing?
zh: 还记得我们建立这个东西、熬夜工作并交付那个东西的时候吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: If it doesn't go anywhere, you don't feel that.
zh: 如果它没有取得任何进展，你就不会有那种感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that's a really important component of this is you need to be winning and succeeding.
zh: 所以我认为这是一个真正重要的组成部分，你需要获胜和成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: One thing that I've learned from Parker, Parker's our CEO at Rippling, he said, "You don't really learn from your mistakes, you learn from your successes."
zh: 我从 Parker 那里学到的一件事是，Parker 是我们 Rippling 的 CEO，他说，“你并没有真正从你的错误中学习，你从你的成功中学习。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like you do, of course, and he would admit you learn a bit from mistakes, but I do think that this is sort of feel good that it's like, "Well, you didn't succeed, but at least you learned something."
zh: 就像你当然会的，他会承认你从错误中学到了一点，但我确实认为这感觉有点像，“好吧，你没有成功，但至少你学到了一些东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I've had failures.
zh: 我有过失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I look back at the nine years I spent working on inkling from day one in 2009 until we sold that business to a private equity firm in 2018, up the curve of Silicon Valley coolness, back down the other side into obscurity.
zh: 当我回想我在 inkling 上花费的九年时间，从 2009 年的第一天开始，直到我们在 2018 年将那项业务出售给一家私募股权公司，沿着硅谷酷炫度曲线上升，再沿着另一侧下降到默默无闻。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, I learned and grew a ton during that time, but in now what I think is six or seven years, I'm trying to do the math, seven years coming up on at Rippling, I've learned so much more because I've seen success.
zh: 当然，我在那段时间里学习和成长了很多，但在我认为的六七年里，我在试图计算，在 Rippling 的七年即将到来时，我学到了更多，因为我见证了成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've seen rapid, wild, crazy off the charts success of the business and it's more informative.
zh: 我见证了业务的快速、疯狂、疯狂的、突破图表的成功，它更有启发性。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's more to glean from seeing how it's done right than there is to glean from seeing how it's done wrong.
zh: 从看到它是如何正确做好的中汲取的东西，比从看到它是如何做错的中汲取的东西更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: If I tell you you're going to get on an airplane and one maintenance technician has seen it done right a hundred times and the other maintenance technician has seen it done wrong a hundred times, but he learned from his mistakes, but still hasn't had any success himself.
zh: 如果我告诉你你要坐上一架飞机，一位维修技术员已经看过它被正确做了一百次，而另一位维修技术员已经看过它被做错了一百次，但他从错误中学习，但他自己还没有取得任何成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, give me a break.
zh: 我的意思是，饶了我吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not even a comparison which plane you're going to feel more comfortable on.
zh: 甚至无法比较你会坐哪架飞机感觉更舒服。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I do think that learning from your mistakes thing is a bit of a feel good trope that actually has very little substance in reality.
zh: 所以我确实认为从错误中学习这件事有点像一个让人感觉良好的陈词滥调，实际上在现实中几乎没有实质内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's why as an early career product manager, or it's why frankly at any stage of your career when you want to learn, you should join a winning team.
zh: 这就是为什么作为早期的产品经理，或者 frankly 在你职业生涯的任何阶段当你想要学习时，你应该加入一个获胜的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's cool to go and start a company at 22.
zh: 22 岁去创办一家公司是很酷的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Good luck to you.
zh: 祝你好运。

speaker: Unknown
en: The odds are not in your favor, but the folks who, when I look at a resume and I see that someone's joined, they were at really good companies when those companies were super exciting and in crazy growth mode.
zh: 成功的几率对你不利，但是那些人，当我查看简历时，我看到有人加入了，他们在那些公司非常令人兴奋且处于疯狂增长模式时，身处那些非常好的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "I instantly want to interview that candidate because I want to hear what they learned from being part of a winning team."
zh: 我就像，“我立刻想面试那位候选人，因为我想听听他们从成为获胜团队的一员中学到了什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's sort of one of my go to heuristics when I'm looking at candidate profiles and I think it's an under-told trope.
zh: 这也是我查看候选人档案时的首选启发式方法之一，我认为这是一个讲述不足的陈词滥调。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sorry, not an under-told trope.
zh: 抱歉，不是一个讲述不足的陈词滥调。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a piece of advice that I don't think people embrace enough in the valley that success begets success and you should chase success.
zh: 这是一个我认为人们在硅谷没有足够拥抱的建议，即成功孕育成功，你应该追求成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of success and learning, you've been a long time COO at Rippling and the reason you're here recently you moved into CPO, chief product officer at Rippling, which is very exciting and very rare.
zh: 说到成功和学习，你一直是 Rippling 的长期 COO，你最近在这里的原因是你转任了 Rippling 的 CPO，即首席产品官，这非常令人兴奋且非常罕见。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't see a lot of COOs moving into product.
zh: 我没看到很多 COO 转向产品领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me ask you why did you move into that role?
zh: 让我问一下你为什么转入那个角色？

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like you've been killing it at COO.
zh: 我觉得你在 COO 的岗位上表现出色。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe that's the reason.
zh: 也许这就是原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Be careful what you're good at.
zh: 小心你所擅长的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also just what are some surprises about this, about moving into product?
zh: 还有一些关于这个，关于转向产品领域的惊喜是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because a lot of people imagine what it's like and then you're actually doing it.
zh: 因为很多人想象它是什么样子的，然后你实际上在做它。

speaker: Unknown
en: The story at Rippling is pretty interesting and I'll tell it because I think it explains why I'm making this transition, but this isn't really about me.
zh: Rippling 的故事相当有趣，我会讲它，因为我认为它解释了我为什么做出这个转变，但这并不是真的关于我。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's sort of a pattern that your listeners would find useful.
zh: 我认为这是你的听众会发现有用的一种模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: In general, your best executives are the ones that you can mostly toss into any challenge and they will bring order to chaos.
zh: 通常，你最好的高管是那些你可以把他们扔进任何挑战，他们会给混乱带来秩序的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They will fix the thing.
zh: 他们会解决问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I do appreciate the terms that people have used at Rippling for me, talking about MacInnis's injured birds, where at any given moment some function is in disarray or in jeopardy and I go and focus very carefully on that function to get it back in order batting 800 maybe, like not always wild success, but I did that everywhere except R&D.
zh: 我确实感激人们在 Rippling 对我使用的术语，谈到 MacInnis 的受伤的鸟，在任何特定时刻某些职能处于混乱或危险之中，我会去非常仔细地关注那个职能，以使其恢复秩序，打击率 800，也许不像总是狂野的成功，但我在除了 R&D 之外的所有地方都这样做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would think about helping out with components of the sales organization like our channel team, or I spent time building out the recruiting function a few times when it needed to be sort of rethought in response to our growth, but it never R&D.
zh: 我会考虑帮助销售组织的组成部分，比如我们的渠道团队，或者我会花时间建立招聘职能几次，当它需要被重新思考以应对我们的增长时，但从来不是 R&D。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I would have my feet up on the table looking out across the floor at this dumpster fire off in the distance, just sort of emitting smoke and wondering if someone was going to go in and deal with that.
zh: 所以我会把脚放在桌子上，看着地板上远处那个垃圾场火灾，只是散发出烟雾，想知道是否有人会进去处理那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the smoke takes various forms and when you're growing as quickly as rippling is growing, it's not always something that necessarily even impacts customers, but it's the sort of thing where you're like, that architecture's not right or they're not measuring adoption correctly."
zh: 烟雾有多种形式，当你的增长像 Rippling 的增长一样快时，它并不一定总是会影响客户，但它是那种你会说，“那个架构不对，或者他们没有正确衡量采用情况。”的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: From the outside, I actually had quite a few criticisms that I could lob in.
zh: 从外部来看，实际上我可以提出相当多的批评。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what happened at Rippling was we made some hiring mistakes.
zh: Rippling 发生的事情是我们犯了一些招聘错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the folks that we had in those roles would agree that they weren't the right people.
zh: 我认为我们担任这些职位的人会同意他们不是合适的人选。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had a hiring mistake in engineering leadership where the product leader at the time had to sort of run engineering.
zh: 我们在工程领导层犯了一个招聘错误，当时的产品领导者必须勉强负责管理工程。

speaker: Unknown
en: We subsequently had a mistake in product hiring and a lot of us had to sort of pitch in.
zh: 随后我们在产品招聘上犯了错误，我们很多人不得不勉强帮忙。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Parker and I sort of stared at each other through two years of this kind of disarray or this chaos or this agony of things and just never really having good executive leadership over both engineering and product at the same time.
zh: Parker 和我互相盯着对方度过了这种混乱或这种混乱或这种痛苦的两年的事情，只是从来没有真正拥有过同时对工程和产品拥有良好的高管领导。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember Parker sort of slumped down in his seat and said, "Oh, I have to run another search."
zh: 我记得 Parker 坐在座位上缩成一团说，“噢，我必须再进行一次搜索。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I said, "No, the gig's up.
zh: 我说，“不，这戏演完了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to go do it."
zh: 我要去做那件事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he really sprung up in his seat.
zh: 他真的在他的座位上弹了起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Really?
zh: 他说，“真的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You'll go do that?"
zh: 你要去那样做吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Dude, this is what the business needs."
zh: 我说，“兄弟，这正是业务所需要的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's what I did.
zh: 所以那就是我所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that really started about a year ago in sort of, I realized I was going to do it and expressed that to Parker in December.
zh: 那真的开始于大约一年前，某种程度上，我意识到我要去做那件事并在十二月向 Parker 表达了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really took it on in January of '25.
zh: 我在 25 年的一月真正接手了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's been 11 months of learning.
zh: 所以这是 11 个月的学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jumping into the product role when the product function itself, although staffed with really talented people, wildly under understaffed, and without a single spiritual leader on top of it to drive consistency and process excellence had become locally optimized but globally incoherent.
zh: 在产品职能本身——尽管配备了真正有才华的人，但人员严重不足，并且没有单一的精神领袖在顶层推动一致性和流程卓越——已经变得局部优化但全局不连贯的情况下——跳进产品角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you know Conway's law, you are destined to ship your org chart.
zh: 如果你知道 Conway 法则，你注定会发布你的组织结构图。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so with a locally optimized, globally incoherent team, you had a locally optimized, globally incoherent product experience that needed to be resolved.
zh: 因此，对于一个局部优化、全局不连贯的团队，你拥有一个局部优化、全局不连贯的产品体验，这需要被解决。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so my efforts over the last 11 months have been to establish greater clarity in terms of how we do things around here, better process, better general leadership, hiring and firing.
zh: 所以我在过去 11 个月的努力是围绕我们在这里如何做事建立更大的清晰度，更好的流程，更好的整体领导力，招聘和解雇。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, just doing the sort of cleanup on aisle three that needed to be done, even though, again, a lot of the people in the team were quite talented and doing an excellent job of managing their specific domains.
zh: 我的意思是，只是做那种需要在第三通道进行的清理工作，即使再次强调，团队中的很多人相当有才华并且在管理他们特定领域方面做得非常出色。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jumping into the product role has been quite eye-opening.
zh: 跳进产品角色已经是非常大开眼界了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel a little bashful about the naivety of my view from the outside a year ago.
zh: 对于一年前我从外观看法的幼稚，我感到有些害羞。

speaker: Unknown
en: Product teams have a hierarchy of needs, and we like to point at the failures to meet elements of that hierarchy higher up the triangle and sort of impugn the failure of that organization for not, as an example, measuring adoption metrics very carefully and not closely tracking those metrics as a means by which to drive execution.
zh: 产品团队有一个需求层次，我们要喜欢指出未能满足该层次更高要素的失败，并某种程度上诋毁该组织的失败，例如，没有非常仔细地衡量采用指标，也没有密切跟踪那些指标作为推动执行的手段。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I jumped in, I was like, "Man, we need to establish some basic standards for test coverage.
zh: 当我跳进去时，我想，“天哪，我们需要为测试覆盖率建立一些基本标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to establish some basic standards for how we do what I call a factory inspection on a product once it's ready to roll off the assembly line."
zh: 我们需要为我们如何做我所谓的‘工厂检查’建立一些基本标准，一旦产品准备好滚下装配线。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Do we have a checklist for what we call product quality and what does product quality mean?
zh: 我们是否有一个检查清单用于我们所谓的‘产品质量’，以及‘产品质量’意味着什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Those basic things weren't there.
zh: 那些基本的东西并不存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the idea that we should be spending time measuring adoption metrics is absolute insanity.
zh: 所以我们应该花时间衡量采用指标的想法简直是疯了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're skipping a lot of steps between here and there.
zh: 你跳过了从这里到那里之间的很多步骤。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we have made great strides and I think it's translating to product quality improvements for our customers, but I feel, as I said, a little dumb for the way I was thinking about it before I jumped into the deep end.
zh: 所以我们已经取得了巨大的进步，我认为这正在转化为我们客户的产品质量提升，但我感觉，正如我所说，对于我在跳进深水区之前的思考方式感到有些愚蠢。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is just no excuse as an executive for sitting outside of the mess and thinking you know the answers.
zh: 身为高管坐在混乱之外并自以为知道答案，是没有任何借口的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a cardinal sin as an executive to do that.
zh: 作为高管那样做是 cardinal sin（大罪/不可饶恕的错误）。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need to go and see.
zh: 你需要去观察。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'd be in the boiler room, you need to study the system, bottom up and develop hypotheses for how to amend the system.
zh: 你必须在锅炉房里，你需要从底层开始研究这个系统，并为如何修正系统 develop（提出）假设。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what I've been doing.
zh: 这就是我所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love hearing this because so many people have teams that are not functioning incredibly well and hearing from someone that is not a longtime product person come in and try to fix these problems, I think is really useful and interesting for people to hear.
zh: 我喜欢听这些，因为很多人拥有功能运转得不是非常好的团队，听一个不是长期产品的人进来并试图解决这些问题，我认为对人们来说听到这些真的很有用且有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: To dig into this a little bit more, was the big lesson and kind of eye-opening moment that there's a lot of foundational work that needs to happen to achieve this outcome that you're trying to achieve, which is measure engagement and adoption well?
zh: 为了更深入地挖掘这一点，最大的教训和令人大开眼界的时刻是，为了实现你试图实现的结果——即很好地衡量参与度和采用度——需要做很多基础工作吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it like tracking and metrics and data science?
zh: 是像跟踪和指标和数据科学吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that kind of the lesson there?
zh: 那是那里的教训吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: The lesson is that everything must be done in its time and order, and you can move really, really quickly.
zh: 教训是，一切都必须按照它的时间和顺序来完成，你可以移动得真的非常快。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no sort of excuse not to move with urgency on all of these things, but you got to do them in order and you have to lead bottom up.
zh: 在所有这些事情上没有理由不带着紧迫感移动，但你必须按顺序做它们，你必须自下而上地领导。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to lead from the specific circumstances you observe.
zh: 你必须根据你观察到的具体情况来领导。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think for me, one of the best things that's happened over the last 11 months is that I've gained a greater trust in my own instincts, that sort of patterns I've matched across other functions do indeed apply in product, but I have both the advantage and disadvantage of not having led a product function before and therefore must think about every problem from first principles.
zh: 我认为对我而言，在过去 11 个月发生的最好的事情之一是，我对我自己的本能获得了更大的信任，那种我在其他职能中匹配的模式确实适用于产品，但我既有也有未领导过产品职能之前的优势和劣势，因此必须从第一性原理思考每一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have no choice.
zh: 我别无选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can read shit on the internet.
zh: 我可以在互联网上读 shit（废话/东西）。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can listen to clear thinkers on topics and import their ideas, but I'm very reluctant to import an idea without breaking it down into its constituent parts and figuring out how it applies at Rippling.
zh: 我可以听话题上的 clear thinkers（清晰思考者）并导入他们的想法，但我非常不愿意导入一个想法，而不将其分解为其组成部分并弄清楚它如何应用于 Rippling。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I don't actually give a shit about adoption metrics as a matter of principle.
zh: 所以实际上我作为原则一点也不在乎采用指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: I care about adoption metrics when I care about adoption metrics.
zh: 我在乎采用指标当我真的在乎采用指标的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: I realize that that's a total logical statement, but it's like I'll get there.
zh: 我意识到那是一个完全符合逻辑的陈述，但就像我会到达那里的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so in certain parts of our product, I really do care about adoption metrics.
zh: 所以在我们产品的某些部分，我真的确实在乎采用指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: I care a lot about adoption metrics in our applicant tracking system, our recruiting product, because it's in a really good place from a usability standpoint, it's very well instrumented, it's got very happy users, it's got an awesome growth profile, and so we should still ...
zh: 我非常在乎我们申请人跟踪系统（ATS），我们的招聘产品中的采用指标，因为它从可用性角度来看处于一个非常好的位置，它有非常好的 instrumented（埋点/观测），它有非常开心的用户，它有一个很棒的增长概况，所以我们应该仍然……

speaker: Unknown
en: It's got an awesome growth profile.
zh: 它有一个很棒的增长概况。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we should be really focused on the adoption metrics because I think that's going to be an important ingredient to low churn over time, removing friction from the implementation process as an example.
zh: 所以我们应该真正专注于采用指标，因为我认为这将是随时间推移低流失率、消除实施过程中摩擦（以此为例）的重要要素。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are other parts of our product where I would say I don't care at all about adoption and am much more focused on foundational things like I said earlier, test coverage or whatever, just to make sure that the thing is stable and good and delivering exactly what it's supposed to deliver once it's adopted.
zh: 我们产品的其他部分，我会说我在乎采用完全不在乎，并且更多地关注基础的事情，正如我之前说的，测试覆盖率或其他什么，只是为了确保事情是稳定的和好的，并且一旦被采用，就精确地交付它应该交付的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now that you're on the inside of the product team, what's something that you think people outside of product, say Matt two years ago or other, I don't know, go to market leads, other execs should hear, need to understand about product that they don't until they're on the inside?
zh: 既然你在产品团队的内部，有一些你认为产品之外的人，比如两年前的 Matt 或其他，我不知道，走向市场的领导者，其他高管应该听到，需要理解关于产品的事情，那些他们直到内部之前都不理解的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll give you another framework that I like to use.
zh: 我会给你另一个我喜欢使用的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the financial world, there's this concept of alpha.
zh: 在金融世界里，有一个叫 alpha 的概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: Alpha is outperformance relative to the index.
zh: Alpha 是相对于指数的超额表现。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's why you have seekingalpha.com as a very popular website.
zh: 这也就是为什么 seekingalpha.com 是一个非常流行的网站。

speaker: Unknown
en: What they mean by that is you're looking to buy something, some combination of assets that will outperform, let's say the S&P 500, if that's your benchmark.
zh: 它们的意思是，你想要购买某种东西，某种资产组合，它会跑赢，比如说标普 500 指数，如果那是你的基准的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: So alpha is the outperformance relative to the index.
zh: 所以 alpha 是相对于指数的超额表现。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you have the concept of beta.
zh: 然后你有 beta 这个概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: Beta is just volatility.
zh: Beta 只是波动性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Beta's not good.
zh: Beta 不是好事。

speaker: Unknown
en: A high beta stock jerks around for no particular reason.
zh: 一只高 beta 的股票毫无缘由地剧烈波动。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's discorrelated with the index.
zh: 它与指数不相关。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very high beta.
zh: 它的 beta 很高。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great if you're an options trader, but other than that, it's not really something you want in an asset.
zh: 如果你是一个期权交易员，这很棒，但除此之外，它并不是你真正想在资产中看到的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So your ideal stock is a very high alpha, very low beta stock.
zh: 所以你理想的股票是高 alpha、低 beta 的股票。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't really come in that shape because alpha and beta tend to be correlated, but that's what you want when you buy a financial asset.
zh: 现实中它们不会以这种形态出现，因为 alpha 和 beta 往往是相关的，但这就是你购买金融资产时想要的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what's the analogy?
zh: 那么类比是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you have high alpha people who are very valuable.
zh: 我认为你有高 alpha 的人，他们非常有价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you also have low beta people who are also very valuable people.
zh: 我认为你也有低 beta 的人，他们也是非常有价值的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Dennis Rodman, basketball player, nut job, very high alpha.
zh: 丹尼斯·罗德曼，篮球运动员，疯子，非常高的 alpha。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's room on every team for one Dennis Rodman is a favorite of mine.
zh: 每个球队都有空间容纳一个丹尼斯·罗德曼，这是我的最爱。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like you can have one difficult employee who's got a ton of upside.
zh: 就像你可以有一个难搞的员工，但他有巨大的上升潜力。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this alpha beta thing, I use it pretty often when contemplating what kind of person I want and also what kind of process I want.
zh: 所以这个 alpha beta 的事情，我在思考我想要什么样的人以及我想要什么样的流程时经常使用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when you're building a product from zero to one, you're probably pursuing alpha.
zh: 所以当你从零到一构建一个产品时，你可能在追求 alpha。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're looking for some angle on this market or this customer problem where the product is actually going to provide an outsized return relative to whatever the default solution is.
zh: 你在寻找在这个市场或这个客户问题上的某个角度，这个产品实际上能相对于默认解决方案提供超额回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you have a more mature product or if you have somebody in the product operations group or whatever, you probably want a more low beta environment where it's like it cranks it out, it does it very reliably.
zh: 当你有一个更成熟的产品，或者如果你有人在产品运营组或任何地方，你可能想要一个更低 beta 的环境，那里就像能稳定产出，非常可靠地完成工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our payroll product, we badly want the payroll product to be very low beta.
zh: 我们的薪资产品，我们非常希望薪资产品是非常低 beta 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We really don't want the payroll product to have any unpredictability or aberration, and so we're willing to accept more process.
zh: 我们真的不希望薪资产品有任何不可预测性或异常，所以我们愿意接受更多的流程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here's a fundamental principle of design in an organization, which is that processes, processes in a business exist for the sole purpose of lowering beta.
zh: 这就是组织设计中的一个基本原则，那就是流程，商业中的流程存在的唯一目的就是降低 beta。

speaker: Unknown
en: Processes are for decreasing volatility in the output of the system.
zh: 流程是为了减少系统产出的波动性。

speaker: Unknown
en: The downside of a process is that it suppresses alpha.
zh: 流程的缺点是它会抑制 alpha。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have to be super, super careful and judicious in the application of process and the product team to know that you're lowering beta in the places where you want to do that without suppressing alpha in the places where you need it.
zh: 你必须非常、非常小心和审慎地在产品团队中应用流程，要知道你是在你想降低 beta 的地方降低 beta，而没有在你需要 alpha 的地方抑制 alpha。

speaker: Unknown
en: So as we've gone through the last year of reforming the way that we build product at Rippling, it's been a game of recognizing those places where I need to implement a touch of process, just a touch.
zh: 所以当我们过去一年在 Rippling 改革构建产品的方式时，这就像是一个识别游戏，识别哪些地方我需要实施一点流程，就一点点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Other places where I need to implement a very clear, rigid process where I don't want alpha, I just want low beta.
zh: 其他地方我需要实施非常清晰、僵化的流程，在那里我不想要 alpha，我只想要低 beta。

speaker: Unknown
en: So examples of this are let's say our product quality list, which we lovingly at Rippling call the PQL.
zh: 这方面的例子比如说我们的产品质量清单，我们在 Rippling 亲切地称之为 PQL。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why PQL?
zh: 为什么要叫 PQL？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so it's actually a really important thing.
zh: 是的，这实际上是一件非常重要的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you want to bring about cultural change in a team ... Look, we have 1,300 people in our R&D organization.
zh: 我认为如果你想在团队中带来文化变革……听着，我们的研发组织里有 1300 人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a big ship that we have to steer.
zh: 我们需要驾驶的是一艘大船。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to create a moment that sticks in people's brains and sort of becomes a zeitgeist or something that they latch onto, you got to create an entity, a vessel for meaning, and then you got to fill that vessel with your meaning.
zh: 如果你想创造一个能留在人们脑中并成为时代精神或他们抓住的东西的时刻，你必须创造一个实体，一个意义的载体，然后你必须用你的意义填满那个载体。

speaker: Unknown
en: A meme, you might say.
zh: 一个模因，你可能会说。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, well, sure, a meme.
zh: 是的，当然，一个模因。

speaker: Unknown
en: A meme is actually a good example of this in common culture.
zh: 模因实际上是大众文化中一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: In pop culture.
zh: 在流行文化中。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's why, when people come to the table with ideas from the outside, I welcome those outside ideas.
zh: 我认为这就是为什么，当人们带着外部的想法来到桌边时，我欢迎那些外部的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the first thing I ask the person to do is to tell me what they mean without using those words.
zh: 但我要求那个人做的第一件事是告诉我他们的意思，而不使用那些词。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when someone comes in and says, "Hey, I want to do this thing on strategy."
zh: 所以当有人进来说，“嘿，我想在战略上做这件事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Cool.
zh: 我会说，“酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell me what you mean without using the word strategy."
zh: 告诉我你的意思，不要用‘战略’这个词。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it forces them to break it down into its constituent parts.
zh: 这迫使他们将其分解为各个组成部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if they can articulate it clearly without using that word, I know that they know what they're talking about.
zh: 如果他们能不用那个词清楚表达，我就知道他们知道自己在说什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if they just fumble around with the word strategy again, I'm like, "Okay, you actually haven't thought this through."
zh: 如果他们只是又在“战略”这个词上胡乱折腾，我就觉得，“好吧，你实际上并没有把这件事想清楚。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So with the PQL, with the product quality list, it's like I could come up with some generic term for this, but I really want a new joiner at the company to understand that this is an idiosyncratic thing to Rippling.
zh: 所以对于 PQL，也就是产品质量清单，就像我可以为此想出一个通用的术语，但我真的希望公司的新人能够理解这是 Rippling 独有的特质。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is unique to us.
zh: 这是我们独有的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You want to understand this thing.
zh: 你需要理解这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also want it to become a component of common parlance in the day-to-day work of the product management and engineering teams.
zh: 我也希望它成为产品管理和工程团队日常工作中通用词汇的一个组成部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So PQL, as cheeky or silly as it sounds, was deliberately sort of angular or stood out as a vessel I could fill with a particular meaning, and so we have a product quality list.
zh: 所以 PQL，无论听起来多么厚脸皮或愚蠢，都是故意设计得有点棱角或突出的，作为一个我可以填充特定含义的载体，所以我们有一个产品质量清单。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the product quality list is lightweight in the sense that it just articulates in the simplest ways the standards we want you to meet when you ship a product.
zh: 而且产品质量清单是轻量级的，因为它只是以最简单的方式阐明了我们在你发布产品时希望你达到的标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't apply to every product, not every line applies to every product, but it's comprehensive and it provides me with a framework for iterating over time as we learn.
zh: 它并不适用于每个产品，并非每一行都适用于每个产品，但它是全面的，并为我提供了一个随着我们学习而随时间迭代的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just yesterday, we shipped the product to Parker.
zh: 就在昨天，我们把产品发布给了 Parker。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is part of our process.
zh: 这是我们流程的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we ship a new product, it goes to Parker, who is the big admin for Rippling at Rippling.
zh: 当我们发布一个新产品时，它会交给 Parker，他是 Rippling 在 Rippling 的大管理员。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're not aware, Parker is the sole payroll administrator for Rippling for all 5,200 employees.
zh: 如果你不知道，Parker 是 Rippling 所有 5,200 名员工唯一的薪资管理员。

speaker: Unknown
en: He personally runs payroll always, there is no exception, for all 5,200 people.
zh: 他总是亲自运行薪资系统，没有任何例外，针对所有 5,200 人。

speaker: Unknown
en: He does complain about it sometimes, but it's a remarkable achievement for the software and perhaps for him.
zh: 他有时会对此抱怨，但这对于软件来说，或许对他个人来说，都是一个了不起的成就。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he also installs any new app that we're going to install for ourselves because we dog food the hell out of everything we build.
zh: 所以他也会安装任何我们要为自己安装的新应用，因为我们会极其大量地「吃自己的狗粮」，也就是试用我们构建的每一个东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yesterday, he goes to install this new application.
zh: 昨天，他去安装这个新的应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're about to ship a new app for feedback, allowing people to give one another feedback on their companies.
zh: 我们正准备发布一个用于反馈的新应用，允许人们在他们的公司内相互给予反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he installs it and he goes in and it dumps them onto an empty screen.
zh: 他安装了它并进入其中，结果它把他甩到了一个空白屏幕上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "What the fuck is this?
zh: 他就像，“这他妈是什么鬼？

speaker: Unknown
en: What is this?
zh: 这是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's going on?
zh: 怎么回事？

speaker: Unknown
en: Hey, wow, talk about fail."
zh: 嘿，哇，真是失败的典型。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I chop another one of my fingers off, I'm down to nine.
zh: 所以我又砍掉了自己的一根手指，我现在只剩下九根了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "Well, what did we miss?"
zh: 我就像，“好吧，我们错过了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: What we missed was there was a fucking feature flag, a fucking feature flag.
zh: 我们错过的是有一个他妈的功能开关，一个他妈的功能开关。

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: It's an extreme standard that might not be achievable, but it's the standard we aspire to.
zh: 这是一个极端的标准，可能无法实现，但这是我们渴望的标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: This framework, the PQL, given these lightweight checklists, iterated on consistently in response to everything we learn as we go, constitutes a very nice lightweight way to lower the beta of the system with hopefully only a modicum of negative impact on the alpha for how we build product.
zh: 这个框架，PQL，鉴于这些轻量级的检查清单，根据我们在沿途学到的一切持续迭代，构成了一个非常不错的轻量级方式来降低系统的贝塔，同时希望对我们如何构建产品的阿尔法只有适度的负面影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: You asked me a very simple question, I gave you a very long-winded answer, but these frameworks help me design systems that scale across one going to 2,000 technical workers.
zh: 你问了我一个非常简单的问题，我给了一个非常冗长的答案，但这些框架帮助我设计系统，该系统能扩展从 1 个到 2,000 个技术工人。

en: Do you get that reference?

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.

en: So this high alpha, low beta, I love this concept.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 是的，我们愿意在这个领域接受一堆波动性，以换取我们从这些人的创造力和冒险精神中获得的 upside，或者那种缺乏流程的东西给了他们做他们想做的事情的余地。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not me, but I adopted it.
zh: 不是我，但我采用了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's that people are smart, passionate, optimistic, tenacious, adaptable, and kind.
zh: 它是说人是聪明的、充满激情的、乐观的、坚韧的、适应力强的和友善的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those five things.

speaker: Unknown
en: Six, can't even count.
zh: 六个，我甚至数不清了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I told you I lost a finger when I made a mistake, so I was down one.

en: Nine to go.

en: SPOTAC isn't by itself a good top down framework, but when you're thinking about, "Oh, why did this candidate just ... Why did it not click?

en: It's that they talked shit about their previous manager and that they were a victim of the performance of their last two companies.

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what it was, they're not optimistic.

speaker: Unknown
en: The framework is super useful to evaluating people.

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the alpha beta framework is also super useful when you come away from a conversation and you're like, "I like that guy.

speaker: Unknown
en: I think he'd be really, really good.

en: And the answer is like, "This is a high alpha product area and he's a low beta person."

speaker: Unknown
en: Valuable, but definitely not the right fit for this.

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think it's really useful in that context as well.

en: I love all these frameworks.

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

speaker: Unknown
en: So high alpha, low beta, sometimes high beta is okay, SPOTAC.
zh: 所以 high alpha，low beta，有时 high beta 也没关系，SPOTAC。

en: In hiring, is there anything else that you find really useful?
zh: 在招聘方面，还有什么你觉得特别有用的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Before we move on to a different topic.
zh: 在我们转向不同话题之前。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I first started working in the product organization, I was introduced to an interview framework or an interview tactic that I hadn't really used much at all, I think in my career, which is that every product person at every seniority level is given the same case study.
zh: 当我刚开始在产品组织工作时，我被引入了一个面试框架或面试战术，这在我的职业生涯中其实没怎么用过，就是每个产品人员，无论什么级别，都得到同样的案例研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the case study is extraordinarily difficult.
zh: 而且那个案例研究非常难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It requires you to think about many, many dimensions simultaneously, to think about data propagation issues.
zh: 它要求你同时考虑很多、很多维度，去思考数据传播问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It gets quite technical.
zh: 它变得相当技术化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the rubric that we use to sort of evaluate performance of that case study is it gives you guidance on what for us, like an entry level PM looks like, what a junior, mid-career, senior executive PM might look like.
zh: 我们用来评估该案例研究表现的评分标准，它为你提供了指导，告诉我们对我们来说，入门级 PM 是什么样的，初级、中级、资深、高管 PM 可能是什么样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everybody comes away from that interview feeling like poop, like they had failed it.
zh: 每个人离开那次面试时都感觉很糟，好像他们失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whereas on our side of it, we're like, "Wow, that person got really far."
zh: 而从我们这边的角度来看，我们就像，"哇，那个人走得真的很远。"

speaker: Unknown
en: They saw around three or four corners in a really impressive way.
zh: 他们以非常令人印象深刻的方式看透了三四个转角。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was 10 they didn't see around, but they saw around four of the hardest ones.
zh: 有 10 个转角他们没看透，但他们看透了其中四个最难的一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they were not defensive when we gave them new information that called into question the validity of their solution.
zh: 当我们给他们新信息质疑他们解决方案的有效性时，他们没有表现出防御性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they were willing to interrupt us to ask more questions," and and and, like a lot of the sort of basic human interaction models.
zh: 而且他们愿意打断我们来问更多问题，"以及以及以及，就像很多那种基本的人类互动模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: You never think that giving someone an impossible task and even including the L5 person versus the VP on the same thing would be productive.

speaker: Unknown
en: And let's just say our recruiting team still sort of kvetches a bit about this and feels like we eliminate people too aggressively at this stage of the interview process.
zh: 我们就这么说吧，我们的招聘团队仍然对这有点抱怨，觉得我们在面试过程的这个阶段淘汰人太激进了。

speaker: Unknown

en: Hand them a drill bit, give them the concrete wall and see if they can get a millimeter or an inch into the concrete.

speaker: Unknown
en: They're never going to get all the way through the wall.
zh: 他们永远无法钻穿整面墙。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't matter.
zh: 没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're going to learn a lot.
zh: 你会学到很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've found that to be kind of an eye-opening new thing for me that has been fun.

speaker: Unknown
en: Look, the joy of product and the joy of product management and the joy of being part of product, I think there's a bunch of joys actually, if I could give you a sort of running list, but one of the big joys is that you get to work with some of the smartest people in software.

speaker: Unknown
en: Engineers are very smart.
zh: 工程师非常聪明。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not always the best sort of social entities.

speaker: Unknown
en: Salespeople are awesome social entities.
zh: 销售人员是很棒的社交实体。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not always the best systems thinkers.

speaker: Unknown
en: You go down the list.

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's kind of a stupid misnomer, but there's some wisdom there.
zh: 我认为这是一个有点愚蠢的误称，但其中有一些智慧。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the wisdom is that you have to be a polymath.
zh: 我认为智慧在于你必须是一个博学家。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've got to be really good at working with other people.

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to be good at communications and articulation.

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to be good at project management.
zh: 你必须擅长项目管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to be good at the science and the math and the engineering.
zh: 你必须擅长科学和数学以及工程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's really fucking cool.
zh: 这真的很他妈酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think one of the great joys of this job for me has been interacting with the tippity top of the smartest and most polymathic people in the industry.
zh: 所以我认为对我来说这份工作的一大乐趣之一就是与行业中最聪明和最博学多才的顶尖人物互动。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll say one other thing about what I love about leading product, which is as a COO, my job was to accept the product as it was and optimize everything around that.
zh: 关于领导产品我喜欢什么，我要说另一件事，那就是作为 COO，我的工作是接受产品本来的样子并优化围绕它的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: My job was to make sure that the product operations, in our business, the interface to the insurance carriers, the interface to the payment entities, the government regulators, that stuff all just sort of worked.
zh: 我的工作是确保产品运营，在我们的业务中，与保险公司的接口，与支付实体的接口，政府监管机构，这些东西都只是正常运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was to make sure that our sales engine, our marketing engine, all the go to-market stuff optimized itself around what the product was.
zh: 这是确保我们的销售引擎、我们的营销引擎，所有进入市场的东西都围绕产品是什么来优化自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was about recruiting and making sure we got people in to work on the product.
zh: 这是关于招聘，并确保我们让人进来从事产品工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: You kind of go down any function that isn't in R&D, and I had some hand in trying to figure out how to make that function work to the best of its ability, given what the product was.

speaker: Unknown
en: And now that I lead product, I'm like, "Oh, wow.
zh: 现在我领导产品，我就像，"哦，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the high order bit."
zh: 这是高位比特。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not that I didn't sort of understand that, but now I really get that product is the high order bit.
zh: 并不是我某种程度不理解那个，但现在我真的明白了产品是高位比特。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you get the product right, it fits in the market, everything else gets easier.

speaker: Unknown
en: Finance is easier, sales is easier, marketing is easier, recruiting is easier, everything gets fucking easier.

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think the other joy of leading the product function is that I get to set the highest order bit in the business's success to one.
zh: 所以我认为领导产品职能的另一个乐趣是我可以把业务成功中的最高位比特设为一。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is really great to hear.

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of times people outside product don't understand these sorts of things and look down on product a lot of times, especially sales folks, COOs a lot of times.

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that you're seeing this and realizing this and recognizing just how important and interesting and challenging this work is and just how awesome PMs are.
zh: 我很高兴你看到这一点并意识到这一点，并认识到这项工作是多么重要和有趣和具有挑战性，以及 PM 有多么棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you know, a lot of people are a very anti-product manager.
zh: 如你所知,很多人非常反产品经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Why do we need product managers?
zh: “我们为什么需要产品经理?

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't need them.
zh: 我们不需要他们。

speaker: Unknown

en: And there's a Chardonnay out there for you."

zh: 你提到了产品市场契合度这一点,我想在这个话题上深入探讨一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've had a couple really interesting experiences of struggling to find product market fit with your own startup.
zh: 你在自己的初创公司寻找产品市场契合度方面有过几次非常有趣的挣扎经历。

speaker: Unknown
en: You said you worked on it for nine years, you said?
zh: 你说你在这方面工作了九年,你是这么说的吗?

speaker: Matt MacInnis
en: Mm-hmm.
zh: 嗯哼。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then with Rippling, complete opposite, extreme product market fit, up and to the right.
zh: 然后对于 Rippling,完全相反,极致的产品市场契合度,一路向上。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's something you've learned about just that that you think people maybe don't understand about what it feels like, what it takes to get to product market fit, how things change?
zh: 关于这一点你学到了什么,你觉得人们可能不太理解它的感觉是什么,达到产品市场契合度需要什么,事情会如何变化?

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a line that this venture capitalist, whose name I will not mention, said, which was that product market fit is a sort of thing where you absolutely know it when you see it, and therefore if you don't absolutely know it, you don't have it.
zh: 有一位风险投资家,我不提他的名字,他说过一句话,产品市场契合度是这样一种东西,你见到它时绝对知道它是,所以如果你绝对不知道它,你就没有它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this kind of gets back to my point about learning from mistakes versus successes.
zh: 这又回到了我从错误中学习还是从成功中学习的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, ah, man, over and over again, over the course of the many years that I spent at Inkling, we thought we had it.
zh: 就像是,啊,伙计,一次又一次,在我在 Inkling 度过的那许多年里,我们以为我们有了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: We thought we had product market fit, maybe, maybe.
zh: 我们以为我们实现了产品市场契合度,也许,也许吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in hindsight, with the benefit of now having experienced solid product market fit, it was so, so obvious that we didn't.
zh: 事后回想,得益于现在体验了稳固的产品市场契合度,那是如此如此明显,我们并没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've invested in like 60 companies or 70 companies.
zh: 我投资了大概 60 家或 70 家公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, it's not something I actively do.
zh: 我不知道,这不是我主动去做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But opportunities, by virtue, I think, of my role at Rippling, sort of show up.
zh: 但机会,我想,得益于我在 Rippling 的角色,就这样出现了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I talk to lots of entrepreneurs and I love it and I find it super stimulating and I love the fresh ideas and it's just something I do as a real cherry on top of the sport that I play already.
zh: 我和很多创业者交谈,我喜欢这样,我觉得这超级令人兴奋,我喜欢新鲜的想法,这只是我在从事的运动之上的锦上添花。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when I get the investor updates for the guys who've been at it for like three, four years and I read the updates from them that I sent to my investors in 2011 and 2012, I'm kind of heartbroken.
zh: 但是当我收到那些已经坚持了三四年的创始人的投资人更新邮件,并读他们在 2011 年和 2012 年发给我投资人的更新时,我有点心碎。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talk in Silicon Valley about never quit, but that is complete absolute venture capital bullshit.
zh: 我们在硅谷谈论永不放弃,但这完全是绝对的风险投资狗屁废话。

speaker: Unknown
en: The incentive of venture capitalist is to put money into your company and milk you dry.
zh: 风险投资家的激励是把钱投入你的公司并把你榨干。

speaker: Unknown
en: They never get their money back.
zh: 他们永远拿不回他们的钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is no way for them to take that investment back.
zh: 他们没有办法拿回那笔投资。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the only logical desire that they would have is for you to keep trying against all odds because there is the occasional example where someone pivoted from A to X and it was wildly different and it worked.
zh: 所以他们唯一的逻辑愿望就是让你不顾一切地继续尝试,因为偶尔会有一些例子,某人从 A 转型到 X,截然不同,并且成功了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Slack was originally some sort of a gaming company and became corporate chat.
zh: Slack 最初是某种游戏公司,后来变成了企业聊天。

speaker: Unknown
en: Airbnb maybe.
zh: 也许还有 Airbnb。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like there's some examples of companies having made wild pivots and succeeded, but man, is that rare.
zh: 是有一些公司进行了疯狂转型并成功的例子,但是,天哪,那是太稀罕了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just so exceedingly rare.
zh: 真的极其稀少。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's important to remember, I'm 45 years old, we're going to be on the planet, the average age of a man in the United States when he dies is something in the mid 70s.
zh: 我认为重要的是要记住,我 45 岁了,我们还将在这个星球上,美国男性的平均死亡年龄是 70 多岁中期。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got 20, 30, maybe if I'm lucky, 40 years left on the planet.
zh: 我在这个星球上还剩 20、30,也许如果我幸运的话,40 年。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very conscious of the time that I have.
zh: 我非常清楚我拥有的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't regret what I did at Inkling, I learned a lot.
zh: 而且我不后悔我在 Inkling 做的事，我学到了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: It informed what I do now.
zh: 这让我明白了我现在在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think the chapter I'm in right now could have come without the chapters before it.
zh: 如果没有之前的那些篇章，我不可能有现在的这一章。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's a beautiful, wonderful thing that I did what I did.
zh: 所以，我做了我做的事，这是一件美好、精彩的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when I read the investor update and I'm like, "You're where I was and you are not getting out of this."
zh: 但是当我读到那份投资人更新时，我会想，“你就在我当年待的地方，而你走不出来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The Silicon Valley try until you die mindset is not pro-entrepreneur, it's pro-venture capitalist.
zh: 硅谷那种“奋斗至死”的心态不是亲创业者的，它是亲风险投资人的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know why that is, but I think it's important to say out loud that you should fucking quit.
zh: 我知道为什么是这样，但我认为大声说出来很重要，你应该他妈的放弃。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should reset the clock, you should reset the cap table because trust me, product market fit when it arrives is insane and it's exciting and you should pursue it.
zh: 你应该重置时钟，你应该重置股权结构表，因为相信我，当产品市场契合度到来时，它是疯狂的且令人兴奋的，你应该去追求它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And never delude yourself into believing you have it when you don't.
zh: 永远不要欺骗自己相信你拥有它，当你实际上并没有的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is dangerous and regrettable.
zh: 这既危险又令人遗憾。

speaker: Unknown
en: How's that for a speech?
zh: 这段演讲怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Beautiful.
zh: 精彩。

speaker: Unknown
en: The anti-VC speech.
zh: 反 VC 演讲。

speaker: Unknown
en: The-
zh: 这个——

speaker: Unknown
en: I got more where that came from.
zh: 我还有更多这种内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, it's not anti-VC.
zh: 顺便说一句，这不是反 VC。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's anti-
zh: 这是反——

speaker: Unknown
en: VC propaganda.
zh: VC 宣传。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody's acting in accordance with their incentives in Silicon Valley, the executives, the founders, the venture ... Everybody's, of course, behaving in accordance with their incentives.
zh: 在硅谷，每个人都在按照各自的激励机制行事，高管们、创始人们、风险投资人们……每个人当然都在按照各自的激励机制行事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the venture capitalists have very strong enduring incentives that have shaped the dynamic of how Silicon Valley works.
zh: 而且风险投资人有非常强烈且持久的激励机制，这些机制塑造了硅谷运作的动态。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's nothing wrong with that.
zh: 那并没有什么错。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just really, really important to point them out and scream at them for the 25-year-old entrepreneur who has no fucking clue how this stuff works.
zh: 指出它们并对着它们大喊大叫对于那个 25 岁的、对这些运作机制毫无头绪的创业者来说，真的非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Trust me, the 45-year-old entrepreneur or the 50-year-old venture capitalist who've been in the game for a while, they get it.
zh: 相信我，那些 45 岁的创业者或 50 岁的、在这个圈子里混迹已久的风险投资人，他们都懂。

speaker: Unknown
en: They've observed it.
zh: 他们已经观察到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They know what it's like.
zh: 他们知道那是什么滋味。

speaker: Unknown
en: The system is there to take advantage of the people who don't, or at least it is the easiest prey for the incentive structures, not for venture capitalists as individual people who are beautiful and all of them are just really wonderful people.
zh: 这个系统的存在就是为了利用那些不懂的人，或者至少对于那些激励机制来说，他们是最容易猎取的对象，而不是针对那些作为个体的、美丽且都很棒的风险投资人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just that the incentive structures lead to some real harm, I think, in certain cases.
zh: 只是在某些情况下，我认为激励机制会导致一些真正的伤害。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the thing I find is when you do quit, VCs ... I'm always just like, "Hey, let me know when you're starting your next thing.
zh: 我发现的一件事是，当你真的放弃时，VC 们……我总是说，“嘿，当你开始做下一件事时告诉我。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm excited to invest."
zh: “我很兴奋投资。”

speaker: Unknown
en: They're rarely, unless they're not a great VC, rarely are they just pissed at you for how could you possibly not make this work [inaudible 00:47:52].
zh: 他们很少——除非他们不是优秀的 VC——很少会仅仅因为你没能做成这件事而生你的气 [听不清 00:47:52]。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the thing, as a founder, when it's time to throw in the towel on your business and you're so obsessed with giving money back to the cap table, I always remind the entrepreneur like, "Hey, if you're in the seed investing game, your forecast is zero.
zh: 关键是，作为创始人，当你到了该向你的生意认输的时候，而你又如此痴迷于把钱还给股权结构表，我总是提醒那位创业者，“嘿，如果你在种子轮投资游戏里，你的预测值是零。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Your assumption on every investment is that it's going to go to zero."
zh: 你对每笔投资的假设都是它会归零。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Any seed investor who doesn't take that stance is off their rocker anyway.
zh: 任何不持有这种立场的种子投资者反正都是疯了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're a very bad investor.
zh: 他们是非常糟糕的投资者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Seek investors who play the long game, who want to be in your second and third company and are willing to take a bet on the first one and let it go to zero so that you can get on with stuff.
zh: 寻找那些打持久战的投资者，那些想要参与你的第二家或第三家公司的人，并愿意在第一家上赌一把并让它归零，这样你就可以继续做别的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is like, I've had that conversation many times.
zh: 这就像，我有过很多次这样的对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by GoFundMe Giving Funds, the zero-fee donor-advised fund.
zh: 本期节目由 GoFundMe Giving Funds 赞助，这是一种零费用的捐赠人建议基金。

en: I want to tell you about a new DAF product that GoFundMe just launched that makes year-end giving easy.

speaker: Unknown
en: GoFundMe Giving Funds is the DAF or donor-advised fund supported by the world's number one giving platform and trusted by over 200 million people.
zh: GoFundMe Giving Funds 是由世界排名第一的捐赠平台支持的 DAF 或捐赠人建议基金，受到超过 2 亿人的信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: You contribute money or appreciated assets like stocks, get the tax deduction right away, potentially reduce capital gains, and then decide later where you want to donate.

speaker: Unknown
en: There are zero admin or asset fees, and you can lock in your deductions now and decide where to give later, which is perfect for year-end giving.
zh: 没有任何管理或资产费用，你可以现在锁定抵扣，稍后决定捐赠去向，这对年终捐赠来说非常完美。

speaker: Unknown
en: Join the GoFundMe community of over 200 million people and start saving money on your tax bill, all while helping the causes that you care about most.
zh: 加入超过 2 亿人的 GoFundMe 社区，开始节省你的税务支出，同时帮助你最关心的事业。

speaker: Unknown
en: Start your giving fund today at gofundme.com/lenny.
zh: 今天在 gofundme.com/lenny 开设你的捐赠基金吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you transfer your existing DAF over, they'll even cover the DAF pay fees.
zh: 如果你转存你现有的 DAF，他们甚至会为你支付 DAF 转账费用。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's gofundme.com/lenny to get started.
zh: 访问 gofundme.com/lenny 即可开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: This begs the question of just when is it time to quit?
zh: 这就引出了一个问题，到底什么时候该放弃？

speaker: Unknown
en: If people hearing this might be like, "Oh man," what are some signs that, okay, it's time to wrap it up?
zh: 如果听到这个的人可能会想，“噢，天哪”，那么有哪些迹象表明，好吧，是时候收手了？

speaker: Unknown
en: Here, look, history provides us with a clear guide.
zh: 瞧，历史为我们提供了一个清晰的指南。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you look at companies having hit it big, they hit big pretty quick.
zh: 当你看那些大获成功的公司时，它们成功得很快。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very, very dangerous to be late to the party, it's very, very dangerous to be early to the party, and the vast majority of the time, that's the problem.
zh: 迟到派对非常非常危险，早到派对也非常非常危险，而且绝大多数时候，这就是问题所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rippling, had it been started in 2014, would not be what it is today.
zh: Rippling，如果是 2014 年创办的，就不会是今天这个样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Rippling, had it been started today, would not be what-
zh: 我认为 Rippling，如果是今天创办的，就不会是今天这个样子——

speaker: Unknown
en: ...Not be what it is today.
zh: ……不会是今天这个样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Rippling, had it been started today, would not be what it is five years from now today, and so I think timing is a lot and it's very hard to control for, but when you get the timing right and the market is real and the product works, product market fit, like I said earlier, it's super clear, and so if I were to pick a number out of a hat just from my lived experience, I think it's very important, one aside, don't ask people for advice, ask people for relevant experience.
zh: 我认为 Rippling，如果是今天创办的，就不会是从现在起五年后的样子，所以我认为时机很重要，而且很难控制，但当时机对、市场真实、产品有效、产品市场契合，就像我之前说的，非常清晰，所以如果我要凭生活经验随便选个数字，我认为很重要，顺便提一句，不要向别人征求建议，要向别人征求相关经验。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you ask them for advice, they will always give it, but if you ask them for relevant experience, they rarely have any to offer, and if they don't have any to offer, then don't ask for their advice.
zh: 如果你向他们征求建议，他们总是会给建议，但如果你向他们征求相关经验，他们很少有能提供的经验，如果他们没有能提供的，那就不要征求他们的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So ask people for relevant experience, and I try to do this with my own entrepreneurs when I work with them, it's like, let me offer you whatever relevant experience I have, and my relevant experience on this topic of when to quit is like, I think we could have called it after the second or third pivot, which was somewhere around year four.
zh: 所以要向别人征求相关经验，我试着和与我共事的企业家这么做，就像是，让我提供我拥有的任何相关经验，而关于“何时退出”这个话题，我的相关经验是，我觉得我们本可以在第二次或第三次转型后结束，那大约是在第四年左右。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is of course very important to believe in what you're building and to be persistent, but there is definitely no shame in saying, "Look, we've pivoted once or twice.
zh: 当然，相信你正在构建的东西并坚持不懈是非常重要的，但说“看，我们已经转型了一两次”，绝对没有任何可耻之处。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not catching.
zh: 它没有起势。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got to go do the next thing," and I think if you're year four, year five in your entrepreneurship journey, and it's not just obviously a screaming rip-roaring growth story, it's extraordinarily difficult.
zh: 我得去做下一件事了”，我认为如果你在创业旅程的第四年、第五年，而且不仅仅是一个显而易见的、疯狂咆哮式的增长故事，那就非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is so extremely rare.
zh: 这极其罕见。

speaker: Unknown
en: So beyond even already the rare scores you're going to face from the outset that now is going to convert to something crazy.
zh: 所以，除了你从一开始就已经要面对的罕见分数之外，现在要转化成某种疯狂的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's hard to hear, I guess, but man, it can be really liberating when you're like, "Fuck it, I'm going to do this.
zh: 所以我想这很难入耳，但是，伙计，当你就像“去他的，我要做这个”时，这真的可以让人解脱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have the energy.
zh: 我有精力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to do it again.
zh: 我要再干一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just going to do it with a clean sheet."
zh: 我要凭一张白纸再干一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is really helpful.
zh: 这真的很有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have this really fun way of describing product market fit around receptors and drugs.

zh: 不要发布产品后发现缺乏成功，然后试图通过营销来度过难关，因为受体结合很可能不存在，对我来说，这是一个非常让人解脱的心态，因为现在我只是需要找到正确的药物，我可以忘掉试图说服身体为我正在构建的任何东西发展出受体结合。
