speaker: Unknown
en: If you abandon a city for a few months, it starts to go fallow, and so entropy is just this concept that shit tends toward disorder.
zh: 如果你把一座城市抛弃几个月，它开始变得荒芜，所以熵就是这个概念，即事情倾向于混乱。

speaker: Unknown
en: The universe, I mean, life itself is a temporary victory against entropy.
zh: 宇宙，我是说，生命本身是对抗熵的暂时性胜利。

speaker: Unknown
en: You and I should not exist.
zh: 你和我本不该存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: The sun gives energy to the planet.
zh: 太阳给予行星能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: It organizes stuff that we can eat and we fight entropy until we lose the battle somewhere, as I said earlier at the age of 70 or 80, if we're lucky.
zh: 它组织我们可以食用的东西，我们与熵对抗，直到我们在某个地方输掉这场战斗，正如我之前所说，在 70 或 80 岁时，如果我们幸运的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does this have to do with product?
zh: 这与产品有什么关系？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is really about effort.
zh: 这实际上是关于努力。

speaker: Unknown
en: The only antidote to entropy, the only antidote to decay in a system is energy.
zh: 对抗熵的唯一解药，对抗系统中衰变的唯一解药就是能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to inject energy.
zh: 你必须注入能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you have a code base, every line of code that you add to that code base increases the entropy of that system and demands ever more energy from human beings to go and intend to it to make sure it doesn't break, and if you want to achieve greatness, if you want an extraordinary outcome, and in particular, if you want to be in the top 10%, top 5% on the X axis so that the Y axis is through the roof, then you have to relentlessly inject energy at every single step of the game.
zh: 所以如果你有一个代码库，你向该代码库添加的每一行代码都会增加该系统的熵，并需要人类投入越来越多的精力去照料它以确保它不会坏掉，如果你想成就伟业，如果你想要一个非凡的成果，特别是，如果你想处于 X 轴的前 10%、前 5%，以便 Y 轴冲上天际，那么你就必须在游戏的每一步无情地注入能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teams will, sadly, but because we are all human, teams will always optimize for local comfort over company outcomes.
zh: 团队会，可悲的是，但因为我们都是人，团队总是会为了局部的舒适度而不是公司的成果而优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not because they get together and think, "We should do that," although unions do do that unequivocally, deliberately, but even in a collection of product managers or engineers, what's going to happen over time is entropy is going to creep in and people are going to optimize for local comfort.
zh: 不是因为他们聚在一起想，“我们应该这样做”，虽然工会确实毫无疑问地、故意地这样做，但即使是在一群产品经理或工程师中，随着时间的推移会发生的是熵会悄悄潜入，人们会为了局部的舒适度而优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your job as an executive, as a leader, is to fight that entropy tooth and nail every single day.
zh: 你作为高管、作为领导者的工作，就是每一天都要竭尽全力对抗那种熵。

speaker: Unknown
en: It means that every time you see a bug, every time you see a bug, not most of the time, every time you go and you drop it at the feet of the product manager or the engineering manager, every time, in public, preferably, it means that every time someone comes to you to hire someone and says that they have skipped the back channel, every time you're like, "You can't hire this person unless you do the back channel," it means that when you get into the board and tired zone on any process, that you as the executive have got to demand the 99th percentile of energy, because otherwise entropy creeps in and the system decays.
zh: 这意味着每次你看到一个 Bug，每次你看到一个 Bug，不是大多数时候，是每次你去把它扔给产品经理或工程经理，每次，最好是在公开场合，这意味着每次有人来找你雇佣某人并说他们跳过了背景调查，每次你都会说，“除非你做背景调查，否则你不能雇佣这个人”，这意味着当你进入任何流程的董事会和疲惫区域时，你作为高管必须要求第 99 百分位的能量，否则熵就会悄悄潜入，系统就会衰变。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to do this.
zh: 你必须得这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the messages that I delivered recently at our big executive big... Like our top 75 manager offsite that we do roughly every 18 months, was a reminder that if the purest form of ambition and the purest and most intense source of energy in the business is the founder CEO, that every next concentric circle of management beyond the founder CEO has the potential to be an order of magnitude drop off in intensity, and that is dangerous because if you go to two layers and it's two orders of magnitude drop off and signal and intensity, that is a very dysfunctional organization.
zh: 我最近在我们的高管大会上传达的一个信息……就像我们大约每 18 个月举办一次的前 75 名管理者场外会议，是要提醒大家，如果说商业中最纯粹的野心形式和最强烈的能量源头是创始人 CEO，那么创始人 CEO 之外的每一层管理圈，其强度都有可能下降一个数量级，这很危险，因为如果你到了两层，而且信号和强度下降了两个数量级，那就是一个非常有功能障碍的组织。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I say to the team was, it's not that you need to buffer people from the intensity of the CEO, it's that you need to absolutely mirror that intensity.
zh: 所以我对团队说的是，问题不在于你需要保护员工免受 CEO 强度的影响，而是你需要完全镜像那种强度。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are plenty of constituents in the business around you who will advocate for relaxing.
zh: 在你周围的企业中，有很多利益相关者会主张放松。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is an infinite supply of people under you who will buffer other team members from the intensity of the demands.
zh: 在你之下，有无限多的人会去缓冲其他团队成员免受需求强度的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your job is not to be one of those buffers.
zh: 你的工作不是成为那些缓冲器之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your job is to preserve that intensity at its highest possible level and let the buffering happen somewhere else, and that's the point is that entropy creeps into the system insidiously and slowly over time off your radar and you have to maintain that intensity every minute of every day to try and fight it if you want to stay at the extreme right end of the power law that obviously governs the outcome of everything that we build.
zh: 你的工作是以尽可能高的水平保持那种强度，并让缓冲发生在别处，关键在于，熵会隐秘且缓慢地随着时间的推移溜进系统中，在你的雷达之外，你必须每一天的每一分钟都保持那种强度来试图对抗它，如果你想保持在幂律分布的最右端，而这显然支配着我们构建的所有事物的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does that look like to pass along that intensity?
zh: 传递那种强度看起来像什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does that feel like?
zh: 那种感觉像什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does that look like?
zh: 那看起来像什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So say Parker comes to you, "This bug sucks.
zh: 比如说 Parker 来找你，“这个 Bug 很烂。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got this broken screen."
zh: 我遇到了这个坏掉的屏幕。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You cut off your finger.
zh: 你切断了你的手指。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe that's the example.
zh: 也许这就是那个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Still got them.
zh: 还留着它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got all 10.
zh: 我 10 个手指都在。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll give you concrete examples.
zh: 我会给你具体的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Actually, the joke that I sort of played on this one when I presented to the team was that the next sort of slide in my presentation was with the sound effect, the Slack huddle thing, and Parker's icon in Slack is just, he uses the generic yellow...
zh: 事实上，当我向团队展示时，我开的一个玩笑是，我的演示文稿中的下一张幻灯片带有音效，就是 Slack 闲聊 (huddle) 的声音，而 Parker 在 Slack 上的头像只是，他使用的是通用的黄色……

speaker: Unknown
en: The egg?
zh: 那个蛋？

speaker: Matt MacInnis
en: Yeah.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 哦，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's funny, and so everybody knows the yellow egg is Parker, and so the next slide in the presentation was boop, boop, boop, boop, boop, boop, boop, which is the sound that Slack makes when someone calls you, and it was Parker Conrad is inviting you to a huddle, and then the next slide was Parker Conrad is modeling personal intensity, and the idea is that if you know, you know, if you've ever been dragged into an... "I want to talk about this fucking problem right now and whatever you're doing, unless it's an interview, I want you to come and have a conversation with me."
zh: 很有趣，所以大家都知道那个黄蛋就是 Parker，所以演示文稿的下一张幻灯片是，嘟、嘟、嘟、嘟、嘟、嘟、嘟，这是有人呼叫你时 Slack 发出的声音，然后是 Parker Conrad 邀请你加入一个闲聊，然后下一张幻灯片是 Parker Conrad 正在亲身示范强度 (modeling personal intensity)，其想法是，如果你懂，你就懂，如果你曾经被拉进过一个……“我现在就想谈谈这个他妈的问题，不管你在做什么，除非是在面试，否则我希望你过来跟我聊聊。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That intensity is one place where it plays out.
zh: 这种强度是它体现出来的一个地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every product team at Rippling, and we have a lot of them now, have public feedback channels.
zh: Rippling 的每个产品团队，我们现在有很多这样的团队，都有公开的反馈渠道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am in there upside down on everything I find when I use those products, and I just model for everybody that this is how at least I want to do it, which is, "I don't like this.
zh: 我就在那里，倒立着展示我在使用那些产品时发现的所有问题，我只是向所有人示范这就是至少我想做的方式，也就是，“我不喜欢这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't understand this.
zh: 我不理解这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell me why this is this way."
zh: 告诉我为什么是这样。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, and people jump in and respond.
zh: 嘣，嘣，嘣，嘣，嘣，然后人们跳进来回应。

speaker: Unknown
en: The factory inspection process that I mentioned, which is where at the end of the assembly line, I jump out with the pickle and we talk about all of the elements.
zh: 我提到的工厂检查流程，就是在装配线的最后，我带着 pickle 跳出来，我们讨论所有的元素。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to record a loom of every major flow through the product.
zh: 你必须为通过产品的每个主要流程录制一个 loom。

speaker: Unknown
en: I personally review every one of those flows, I got a backlog I got to catch up on today and provide feedback to people always in a public channel so that every other product manager and engineering manager can jump in and see how the process has worked for other teams.
zh: 我个人审查每一个流程，我今天有一个待办清单要赶上，并且总是在公开频道提供反馈，以便其他所有产品经理和工程经理都可以跳进来，看看该流程是如何为其他团队工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's about modeling the intensity publicly so that other people can say, "Okay, this is how we do things around here," and it gives the reaction from the team that received the message that you have to inject that energy every minute of every day and that there are no exceptions, was not like, "Ooh, that's exhausting."
zh: 这是关于公开地示范强度，以便其他人可以说，“好的，这就是我们在这里做事的方式，”这给了收到该信息的团队一种反应，你必须每一天的每一分钟都注入那种能量，并且没有任何例外，这种反应并不是，“噢，真累人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The reaction is, "Oh, that is so invigorating."
zh: 反而是，“噢，那是多么令人振奋。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so wonderful to hear that we're going to achieve these great outcomes, or at least we have a shot at doing so, and that you're empowering me to follow my instinct, which is to really push for the best result.
zh: 听到我们要实现这些伟大的结果真是太好了，或者至少我们有机会这样做，而且你正在赋权让我跟随我的直觉，那就是真正争取最好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: The reaction universally is like, "Ugh, what a relief that I get to go be intense," because nobody in a position of leadership wants to be chill, and what's worse than a chill boss?
zh: 普遍的反应就像是，“唉，真是一种解脱，我就能去变得强烈了”，因为处于领导地位的人没人想表现得很淡定 (chill)，还有什么比一个淡定的老板更糟糕的呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, don't work for a chill boss.
zh: 不，不要为一个淡定的老板工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't be a chill boss.
zh: 不要做一个淡定的老板。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the most pejorative label I could give you.
zh: 这是我能给你的最具贬义的标签。

speaker: Unknown
en: Chill boss or chill PM.
zh: 淡定的老板或淡定的产品经理 (PM)。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't be chill.
zh: 不要淡定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Chill doesn't accomplish shit.
zh: 淡定啥也干不成。

speaker: Unknown
en: Be intense.
zh: 要强烈。 [[743】 要优秀，要尊重人，要强烈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Be good, be respectful, be intense.
zh: 做好人，保持尊重，保持激情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't be chill.
zh: 不要淡定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, this advice comes from where we started, which is this is what success looks like, because somebody that is less chill than you is going to find that crack and come after your market.
zh: 再次强调，这个建议来源于我们的起点，这就是成功看起来像什么的样子，因为那个比你没那么淡定的人会找到那个裂缝并来抢占你的市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the gist?
zh: 这是主旨吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: For sure.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, again, if you're chill and you move the X axis down 10 or 20 points, the Y axis collapses.
zh: 我的意思是，同样，如果你很淡定，并且你把 X 轴向下移动 10 或 20 点，Y 轴就会崩塌。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't just drop 10 or 20%.
zh: 它不仅仅是下降 10% 或 20%。

speaker: Unknown
en: The Y axis collapses, and this is just kind of true in everything we do.
zh: Y 轴塌陷了，这在我们做的所有事情中都是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, if you want to win, you should probably try.
zh: 所以是的，如果你想赢，你可能应该尝试一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what I always say to people trying to build lifestyle businesses.
zh: 这就是我总是对那些试图建立生活方式型生意的人说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's always this idea, "I'm going to build a side business, I'm going to make recurring income, it's going to be amazing," and in my experience, anytime there's a market or something shows up that's juicy and there's ways to make money, somebody's going to come at you and work harder, raise more money, and there's only so long you can maintain that.
zh: 总是有这种想法，“我要建立一个副业，我要获得经常性收入，这会很棒”，而根据我的经验，每当出现一个市场或某种有利可图的东西并且有赚钱的方法时，就会有人来和你竞争，他们工作更努力，筹集更多资金，而你只能维持那么长时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, man, there's a whole other podcast episode on the concept of leverage.
zh: 好吧，伙计，关于杠杆的概念，这足以做另一期播客节目了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you sell your time, you've only got 24 hours a day to give, but if you can create a product that scales, the marginal cost of a unit of that product is zero like software, it's going to be competitive, man.
zh: 如果你出卖你的时间，你每天只有 24 小时可以给予，但如果你能创造一个可扩展的产品，该产品的边际成本为零，比如软件，那将是具有竞争力的，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sell your time, it's not going to be super competitive, but achieve that level of leverage and it's a pretty efficient market.
zh: 出卖你的时间，不会具有超级竞争力，但一旦达到那种杠杆水平，这就成了一个非常高效的市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: To close out this thread of intensity and adding energy to everything, something I've heard about you is you're big on escalating.
zh: 为了结束关于强度和给一切事物增加能量这个话题，我听说的一件事是你非常注重升级事态。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a big fan of escalating issues, and also you always tell people to never not give you negative feedback, to always share feedback to not hide anything.
zh: 你是升级问题的大力支持者，而且你也总是告诉人们不要不给你负面反馈，要总是分享反馈，不要隐瞒任何事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell me about those two.
zh: 跟我讲讲这两点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fundamentally, the most selfish thing you can do is withhold feedback from someone.
zh: 从根本上说，你能做的最自私的事情就是隐瞒对某人的反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who are you optimizing for when you do that?
zh: 当你这样做时，你是为谁优化？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you optimizing for when you think a thought that would help someone improve and you avoid giving it to them because it would make you uncomfortable?
zh: 当你产生一个想法，这个想法能帮助某人改进，但你因为会让你感到不舒服而避免告诉他们时，你在优化什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, you're optimizing for your own comfort and it's fundamentally selfish.
zh: 好吧，你在优化你自己的舒适度，这在根本上是自私的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the most selfish thing you could possibly do, is withhold feedback that would otherwise be useful to someone because you don't want to be uncomfortable.
zh: 你可能做的最自私的事情，就是隐瞒原本对某人有用的反馈，因为你不想感到不舒服。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, that's not how high performance teams operate.
zh: 嗯，这不是高绩效团队运作的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I demand feedback, and I give it, and it doesn't mean that when I give feedback, it's not open to being questioned or discussed.
zh: 所以我要求反馈，我也给出反馈，这并不意味着当我给出反馈时，它不接受质疑或讨论。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, of course it is, but when I observe something, I try to say it.
zh: 我的意思是，当然可以，但当我观察到某事时，我会尽量说出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the feedback topic.
zh: 这就是反馈这个话题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The part of this that has been interesting to me is that people withhold, escalate, the customers withhold.
zh: 这部分让我感兴趣的是，人们会隐瞒，升级事态，客户会隐瞒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Customers don't want to escalate to me as an executive.
zh: 客户不想向我这样的高管升级事态。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even the founders in whose businesses I've invested who use Rippling are reluctant to hit me up when something goes wrong because they don't want to bother me, but it's literally my job, literally my job to find things, problems, and make them better, and by virtue of making those specific things better, to iterate on the processes so that the system that builds the system can get better.
zh: 即使是我投资了其业务的使用 Rippling 的创始人，在出现问题时也不愿意联系我，因为他们不想打扰我，但这字面上就是我的工作，字面上就是我的工作，去发现问题、问题，并让它们变得更好，并通过让那些具体的事情变得更好，来迭代流程，以便构建系统的系统可以变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no greater gift to me as a product executive than receiving an escalation from a customer.
zh: 作为一名产品高管，对我来说没有比收到客户的事态升级更大的礼物了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have an escalations team at Rippling, which sounds weird, but it's people who are just particularly skilled at pistol whipping other people in the company to get to real root causes, real root causes.
zh: 我们在 Rippling 有一个升级团队，这听起来很奇怪，但这些人是特别擅长用鞭子抽公司里的其他人，以找到真正的根本原因，真正的根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not like throw away the word root cause, like, "Oh, we fixed the data error and shut the ticket down."
zh: 不是像丢弃根本原因这个词那样，比如，“哦，我们修复了数据错误并关闭了工单。”

speaker: Unknown
en: No, you went and you found the software that created the data error, and then you found the system that created the software that created the data error, and you solved all of that back to the top.
zh: 不，你去找到产生数据错误的软件，然后你找到产生产生数据错误的软件的系统，然后你把所有这些都一直解决到顶层。

speaker: Unknown
en: Escalation seems extremely good at that at Rippling.
zh: 升级事态在 Rippling 似乎对此非常有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have sort of a dedicated little team that does this for us.
zh: 所以我们有一个专门的小团队为我们做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Escalations are a gift, and it's like, if you're a listener right now on this podcast and you are a Rippling customer and you have shit that you think we should know, the fact that I might already know it is not a reason for you to withhold the gift of your feedback.
zh: 升级是一种礼物，这就像是，如果你现在是这个播客的听众并且你是 Rippling 的客户，并且你有你认为我们应该知道的事情，仅仅是我可能已经知道了并不是你隐瞒反馈礼物的理由。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's an attitude that I take to this every day.
zh: 这是我每天对待这件事的态度。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got a little cue of some stuff that I've... Minor things that are from the last couple of days from people who had some knits and issues that I'm just like, I've got them queued up on my to do list today and I'm going to take them to the product teams directly and be like, "I'd like to understand what happened here."
zh: 我有一些小贴士，关于我过去几天的一些小事情，来自那些有一些挑剔和问题的人，我只是把他们排在我今天的待办事项清单上，我将直接把他们带到产品团队那里，就像，“我想了解这里发生了什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Not in a negative way.
zh: 不是以消极的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just think we'll all get better if we study this one, and so yeah, escalations are a gift, feedback like that is a gift, and nobody is ever inconveniencing me when they do it.
zh: 我只是认为，如果我们研究这个，我们都会变得更好，所以是的，升级是礼物，那样的反馈是礼物，当他们这样做时，没有人是在打扰我。

speaker: Unknown
en: For people that are listening to this and feeling like, "Man, this is so stressful and intense and just like, I don't know if I want to work this way," give them a sense of just how successful Rippling is.
zh: 对于那些正在听这个并且感觉像，“伙计，这太有压力了，太紧张了，就像，我不知道我是否想这样工作”的人，给他们一种感觉，Rippling 有多么成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a lot of people may not even have heard of Rippling.
zh: 我想很多人甚至可能没听说过 Rippling。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people are like, "Yeah, it's doing great."
zh: 很多人会说，“是的，它做得很好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What are some things you can share that are public or not public that give people a sense of just how massive this business has become?
zh: 你可以分享哪些公开的或不公开的事情，让人们感受到这项业务变得多么巨大？

speaker: Unknown
en: People look at Rippling from the outside, I think they think of us as payroll and HR or whatever, which is cool.
zh: 人们从外部看 Rippling，我认为他们把我们看作是薪资和人力资源之类的，这很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a bit like saying Microsoft is a desktop operating system company or it's like every company starts from somewhere.
zh: 这有点像说微软是一家桌面操作系统公司，或者就像每家公司都是从某个地方开始的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... system company or ... It's like every company starts from somewhere and grows out from there.
zh: ...系统公司或者...这就像每家公司都是从某个地方开始并从那里向外发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: We see ourselves as building the most successful business software platform in history.
zh: 我们将自己视为正在建立历史上最成功的商业软件平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, that's the mission statement of our product organization under the umbrella of the mission statement of the company, which is to free smart people to work on hard problems.
zh: 事实上，这是我们产品组织的使命宣言，是在公司使命宣言的伞下，公司的使命宣言是让聪明的人去处理难题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you translate that from where we are today to where we think we're taking things, it's like we really do believe that the core of every workflow and everything that a company has to do, be it AI or manual traditional GUI based software, is who's doing stuff, who owns it, who's accountable?
zh: 当你将其从我们今天的位置转化为我们要带事情去的地方时，就像我们真的相信，每个工作流的核心和一家公司必须做的一切，无论是 AI 还是传统的基于 GUI 的软件，都是谁在做事情，谁拥有它，谁负责？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the people record is a really important component of that.
zh: 所以人员记录是其中非常重要的组成部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can argue that the customer record's also very important.
zh: 你可以争辩说客户记录也非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course some big businesses have been built on that primitive as well, but we think the people primitive is actually much more important.
zh: 当然，一些大业务也是建立在这个基础上的，但我们认为人员基础实际上更重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that the only thing that hasn't been done here is somebody hasn't been ambitious enough to build a good business on top of that primitive.
zh: 而这里唯一没做过的事情就是没有人有足够的雄心在这个基础之上建立一个好的业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: Workday is terrible software.
zh: Workday 是糟糕的软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody agrees on that.
zh: 大家都同意这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Workday agrees on that.
zh: 我认为 Workday 也同意这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Good luck to them.
zh: 祝他们好运。

speaker: Unknown
en: But they have failed actually, despite their success, to build a really broad general purpose software platform for business software on the foundation of the people primitive.
zh: 但实际上，尽管他们很成功，却未能基于人员这一原语，为商业软件构建一个真正广泛的通用软件平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're going to do that.
zh: 所以我们要做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're successful because we deliver on that promise at the scale we're at today.
zh: 我们之所以成功，是因为我们以今天的规模兑现了那个承诺。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fact that you can do ... and this is not a sales pitch or sort of like an advertisement for Rippling.
zh: 事实上你可以做……这不是推销，也不像是给 Rippling 打广告。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just think it's important to sort of contemplate that when you bring together a bunch of disparate business processes into one system on a common business data graph, an object graph, a data lake with a consistent interface, you can do some pretty magical things.
zh: 我只是认为思考这个问题很重要，当你把一堆截然不同的业务流程整合到一个系统中，基于一个共同的业务数据图、一个对象图、一个具有一致接口的数据湖时，你可以做一些非常神奇的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we do payroll, we do HCM, we do IT, we do spend.
zh: 所以我们做薪酬，我们做 HCM，我们做 IT，我们做支出。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are about to launch a new product in the category of business intelligence and data management.
zh: 我们即将在商业智能和数据管理领域发布一款新产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a whole bunch of other stuff coming beyond that.
zh: 除此之外，还有一大堆东西正在推出。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you layer in AI on top of this, where we alone, where we alone have all of your business data organized into this nice, neat package with a beautiful semantic layer on top of it.
zh: 然后你在其之上加入 AI，只有我们，只有我们将你所有的业务数据整理成这个整洁的包裹，并在其上有一个漂亮的语义层。

speaker: Unknown
en: The AI can work magic.
zh: AI 就可以施展魔法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we have shipped nothing, nothing yet in this category that I would say gets anywhere near what we're going to show next year.
zh: 而在这个类别中，我们还没有发布任何东西，没有任何东西能接近我们明年要展示的水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it is going to set the standard.
zh: 它将树立标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is going to be the most ... The back flips that the AI is doing reading and writing data for the user just on our internal use cases at Rippling is jaw dropping.
zh: 它将是最……AI 在仅为我们在 Rippling 的内部用例读写数据时所做的后空翻，简直令人瞠目结舌。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm super excited about the tailwind this is going to create for us.
zh: 所以我对这将为我们带来的顺风感到超级兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: You ask about what can I share about the success of the company?
zh: 你问我可以分享关于公司成功的什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What I can say, there are tens of thousands of companies that now run on Rippling.
zh: 我可以说，现在有数以万计的公司在使用 Rippling 运营。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're less than 1% of the market.
zh: 我们还不到市场的 1%。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the market cap at 16 billion, I think now undervalues where we are from a revenue perspective by a long shot.
zh: 而 160 亿的市值，我认为现在从收入角度来看大大低估了我们的位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is just so much upside to do what we're doing.
zh: 做我们在做的事还有太多的上行空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And SaaS might be dead-ish in terms of point solutions, but long live SaaS in terms of what we're building.
zh: 就单点解决方案而言，SaaS 可能是半死不活了，但就我们正在构建的东西而言，SaaS 万岁。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me follow that thread in AI.
zh: 让我顺着 AI 这个思路讲。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's been a lot of talk about AI is going to replace SaaS, as you maybe just said.
zh: 有很多关于 AI 将取代 SaaS 的讨论，就像你可能刚才说的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are going to vibe code their way to their payroll system, which I ... good luck to the employees of those companies.
zh: 人们将会一路 vibe code 到他们的薪酬系统，我……祝那些公司的员工好运。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what I'm hearing here is that you actually do believe a lot of SaaS companies that are selling individual solutions are in big trouble.
zh: 所以我在这里听到的是，你实际上确实相信许多销售单一解决方案的 SaaS 公司正处于大麻烦之中。

speaker: Unknown
en: The answer implied here is this kind of compound startup idea of you need to do a lot of things for people for them to continue to pay for your software.
zh: 这里隐含的答案是这种复合型创业公司的想法，即你需要为人们做很多事情，才能让他们继续为你的软件付费。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the gist?
zh: 这是主旨吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the gist is ... there's a really good quote.
zh: 我认为主旨是……有一句非常好的名言。

speaker: Unknown
en: I forget who it's attributable to, but it's, "There's two ways to make money in software, bundling and unbundling."
zh: 我忘了是谁说的了，它是，“在软件中有两种赚钱方式，捆绑和解绑。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you just got to get the timing right.
zh: 你只需要把时机搞对。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is a period of bundling.
zh: 所以这是一个捆绑的时期。

speaker: Unknown
en: So here's the problem; point solutions don't have enough data in the age of AI to be useful.
zh: 所以问题在于；在 AI 时代，单点解决方案没有足够的数据来发挥作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to be able to provide the AI with a lot of context about a lot of data so it can do things.
zh: 你必须能够为 AI 提供关于大量数据的丰富上下文，这样它才能做事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can do joins.
zh: 它可以做连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can do correlations.
zh: 它可以做关联。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you're a point solution, you're in hard water because you've got to now rely on data from other sources.
zh: 所以如果你是一个单点解决方案，你就处于困境中，因为你现在必须依赖来自其他来源的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've got to integrate to third party systems.
zh: 你必须集成到第三方系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you integrate to a third party system, even the best ones are still sort of drinking their data through a straw, which is a real problem.
zh: 当你集成到第三方系统时，即使是最好的系统也仍然像是在通过吸管喝他们的数据，这是一个真正的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, the biggest HCM software company you can think of integrates to the other biggest payroll software company you can think of through flat files via SFTP.
zh: 我的意思是，你能想到的最大的 HCM 软件公司与你所能想到的最大的薪酬软件公司集成，是通过 SFTP 传输平面文件来实现的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are they going to do?
zh: 他们能做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are they going to do?
zh: 他们能做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just never going to work.
zh: 这永远行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just no way.
zh: 绝对没门。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I literally have no idea what they're going to do.
zh: 所以我真的不知道他们要做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're just not going to build AI software, I guess.
zh: 我想他们只是不会构建 AI 软件了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Point SaaS is sort of in a rough spot, especially when you cleave two really important systems apart and say they have to integrate.
zh: 单点 SaaS 处境有点艰难，尤其是当你把两个真正重要的系统分开并说它们必须集成时。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very, very hard.
zh: 这非常非常难。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other thing that I would say about the world of, even just like not SaaS, but AI software is that point solutions in the AI world are also in a rough spot for the same reason.
zh: 关于这个世界的另一件事，哪怕不只是 SaaS，而是 AI 软件，那就是 AI 世界中的单点解决方案也因为同样的原因处境艰难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like if you're selling the shovels like OpenAI and Google with Gemini, you can make money.
zh: 这就像如果你在卖铲子，像 OpenAI 和 Google 用 Gemini，你可以赚钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you own the mine, like Rippling, with the data, you can make money.
zh: 如果你拥有矿，像 Rippling，拥有数据，你可以赚钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're somewhere in the middle, building AI software that then tries to use the shovels from the shovel provider, but then also needs to rent out the mine, or get the ore out of somebody else's mind and start refining it, you're in a very difficult place from an economic standpoint.
zh: 如果你处于中间地带，构建 AI 软件，然后试图使用铲子提供商的铲子，但还需要租用矿山，或者从别人的矿里提取矿石并开始提炼，从经济角度来看，你处于一个非常困难的境地。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you're not going to be permitted by either of those parties to build a big business on their backs.
zh: 因为那双方都不会允许你在他们的背上建立一个大生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's just not how it's going to work.
zh: 那是行不通的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're going to demand value capture that crushes your unit economics.
zh: 他们会攫取价值，压垮你的单体经济效益。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I look at the landscape of AI companies that I've seen and I think you have to have a really durable, interesting insight that gives you a shot at viable unit economics to be an interesting business.
zh: 所以我看我所见过的 AI 公司格局，我认为你必须有一个真正持久、有趣的洞察，让你有机会获得可行的单体经济效益，才能成为一个有趣的生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is going to kill off 80 or 90% of the stuff that I see right now as standalone AI businesses.
zh: 这将会扼杀我现在看到的 80% 或 90% 作为独立 AI 业务的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I'm hearing here ... I love that you correct me when I get these things wrong, and that's exactly what I want.
zh: 所以我在这里听到的……我喜欢你在我把这些弄错时纠正我，这正是我想要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I'm hearing is it's less about how difficult it is to build the SaaS product.
zh: 我听到的是，这与其说是关于构建 SaaS 产品有多难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's about, do you have first party data that allows you to build an incredible AI product on top of what you've got?
zh: 不如说是，你是否拥有第一方数据，让你能够在你拥有的东西之上构建一个令人难以置信的 AI 产品？

speaker: Unknown
en: Your database?
zh: 你的数据库？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Changing values in your database, that's what it does.
zh: 改变你数据库中的值，这就是它所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a really hard problem.
zh: 这真的是一个很难的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of Rippling's superpowers is we're a coin sorter.
zh: Rippling 的超能力之一是我们是一个硬币分拣机。

speaker: Unknown
en: You dump $20,000 for an employee in the top of the coin sorter, and it figures out what goes to the government, what goes to health insurance, what goes to your 401k, what goes to you.
zh: 你把一名员工的 20,000 美元倒进硬币分拣机的顶部，它会弄清楚哪些给政府，哪些给健康保险，哪些给你的 401k，哪些给你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it has to move all that money very, very reliably and seamlessly, very challenging software to build and manage.
zh: 它必须非常非常可靠且无缝地移动所有这些钱，构建和管理起来非常有挑战性的软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's it doing?
zh: 它在做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Even that is just flipping bits in a database.
zh: 即使那样也只是在数据库中翻转比特。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so AI is a new way to flip bits.
zh: 所以 AI 是一种翻转比特的新方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is just a new way to flip bits.
zh: AI 只是一种翻转比特的新方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hopefully a way that abstracts us a little bit further from having to think because our future is Wally.
zh: 希望这种方式能让我们稍微进一步地从必须思考中抽象出来，因为我们的未来是 Wally。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be great.
zh: 这将会很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of Wally, actually, I have this Matic Robot.
zh: 说到 Wally，其实，我有一个 Matic 机器人。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have one of these?

speaker: Unknown
en: Uh-huh.
zh: 嗯。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a self-driving car robot, basically, a self-driving car.
zh: 它基本上就像一辆自动驾驶汽车机器人，一辆自动驾驶汽车。

speaker: Unknown
en: People built this robot that cleans your house.
zh: 人们构建了这个可以打扫你房子的机器人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe I didn't mention that.
zh: 也许我没提到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a house cleaning robot that just goes around.

en: Oh, okay.

en: It's like a Roomba.

en: You should have one.

en: They're expensive, but incredibly cool.

speaker: Unknown
en: And actually in Wally, there's a scene where they actually have basically what these things look like.
zh: 而且实际上在 Wally 里，有一幕他们基本上有这些东西的样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's not so far-fetched for that movie.
zh: 所以对于那部电影来说这并不那么牵强。

speaker: Unknown
en: I definitely was actually quite prescient, perhaps with the exception of the gravity defying day beds.
zh: 我实际上确实非常有先见之明，也许除了那些反重力的日间床。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, but that's not good news.
zh: 我不知道，但这不是好消息。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've seen that movie.
zh: 你看过那部电影。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: So on this AI stuff, which I'm hearing is we're going to see a lot more consolidation where these point solution companies realize they need this data.
zh: 所以关于这个 AI 的事情，我听到的是我们会看到更多的整合，这些单点解决方案公司意识到它们需要这些数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is existential, and they're going to combine and merge and bundle, as you described?
zh: 这是生死攸关的，它们会像你描述的那样结合、合并和捆绑吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's possible.
zh: 这是可能的。

speaker: Lenny
en: Or for now.

speaker: Unknown
en: I am not an investigator on this stuff.
zh: 我不是这件事的调查员。

en: I think there's some really interesting investors out there who I think are thinking quite deeply about this.
zh: 我认为有一些非常有趣的投资者，我认为他们在深入思考这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in particular, the conviction, which is like Mike Fornell and Sarah Guo.
zh: 特别是那种信念，就像 Mike Fornell 和 Sarah Guo 那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think those are two investors who are hyper focused on AI.
zh: 我认为他们是两位极度专注于 AI 的投资者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when they made the decision to take that approach, at the time I thought that's kind of narrow.
zh: 当他们决定采取那种方法时，当时我觉得那种做法有点狭隘。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now I'm like, no, no, no, that was the right move.

speaker: Unknown
en: And it just means that they have a very deep, deep, deep set of hypotheses that they've formed over the course of seeing every AI deal in the valley.
